description: ### **Minimum qualifications:** * Bachelor's degree or equivalent practical experience. * Experience in digital sales, client management, customer service, business development, customer outreach, marketing, consulting, digital advertising, or a related field. * Ability to communicate in English and Danish fluently to support client relationship management in this region. ### **Preferred qualifications:** * Familiarity with Google Ads and online advertising, with Google Digital Workshop certification or equivalent experience. * Ability to own a portfolio in an advertising or media business context in Denmark, and consistently overachieve quotas to drive growth. * Ability to build compelling narratives and utilize storytelling as a client engagement strategy. * Ability to influence stakeholders internally and externally to deliver impact. * Ability to multi\-task and manage multiple projects at a time while paying attention to detail. **About the job** ----------------- Consulting with a wide variety of existing advertising companies of all sizes, you are committed to finding the optimal advertising solution for each client. Your interest in sales and the digital economy and your entrepreneurial drive enables brands to expand their businesses with Google's latest advertising products and solutions. Your understanding of online media combined with your commercial know\-how shape how new and existing businesses grow. Using your effective communication skills, you are the face of Google to a portfolio of clients whose needs you listen to, and help define the strategy for how their products and services can reach thousands or millions of users.Google Customer Solutions (GCS) sales teams are trusted advisors and competitive sellers who maintain a relentless focus on customer success by bringing the best Google has to offer to small\- and medium\-sized businesses (SMBs), which are the backbone of our communities. As a member of our team, you’ll have the opportunity to work with company owners and make a real difference in their businesses by helping them grow. Together, we help shape the future of innovation for customers, partners, and sellers...and we have fun doing it.Individual pay is determined by factors including job\-related skills, experience, and relevant education or training. Ireland: €60000 \- €61500 (EUR) \+ 30% bonus target \+ equity \+ benefits Learn more about benefits at Google.**Responsibilities** -------------------- * Serve as a trusted advisor to clients by building relationships with key decision\-makers and business owners to understand their goals and digital marketing strategies, empowering their business with Google advertising. * Manage large accounts and identify opportunities where Google Ads can impact growth while tracking account growth and product adoption quarterly goals. * Conduct frequent phone consultations to evaluate campaign performance, make account recommendations, and mentor/influence business owners on digital marketing strategy. * Engage in essential activities to drive customer relationship growth (e.g., proactive engagement, campaign performance review, client meetings, pitches, research, internal strategy building/execution, etc.). * Participate in training throughout the program to develop professional and business skills. Google is proud to be an equal opportunity workplace and is an affirmative action employer. We are committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, gender identity or Veteran status. We also consider qualified applicants regardless of criminal histories, consistent with legal requirements. See also Google's EEO Policy and EEO is the Law. title: Customer Support Engineer
description: **Company Overview** KLA is a global leader in diversified electronics for the semiconductor manufacturing ecosystem. Virtually every electronic device in the world is produced using our technologies. No laptop, smartphone, wearable device, voice\-controlled gadget, flexible screen, VR device or smart car would have made it into your hands without us. KLA invents systems and solutions for the manufacturing of wafers and reticles, integrated circuits, packaging, printed circuit boards and flat panel displays. The innovative ideas and devices that are advancing humanity all begin with inspiration, research and development. KLA focuses more than average on innovation and we invest 15% of sales back into R\&D. Our expert teams of physicists, engineers, data scientists and problem\-solvers work together with the world’s leading technology providers to accelerate the delivery of tomorrow’s electronic devices. Life here is exciting and our teams thrive on tackling really hard problems. There is never a dull moment with us. **Group/Division** The KLA Services team headquartered in Milpitas, CA is our service organization that consists of Service Sales and Marketing, Spares Supply Chain management, Field Operations, Engineering, Product Training, and Technical Support. The KLA Services organization partners with our field teams and customers in all business sectors to maintain the high performance and productivity of our products through a flexible portfolio of services. Our comprehensive services include: proactive management of tools to identify and improve performance; expertise in optics, image processing and motion control with worldwide service engineers, 24/7 technical support teams and knowledge management systems; and an extensive parts network to ensure worldwide availability of parts. **Job Description/Preferred Qualifications** We are looking for a talented **Customer Support Engineer** based in our Dublin, Ireland location. Major Responsibilities include: * Customer service activities associated with updating, troubleshooting, diagnosing and repairing complex electro\-optical and electro\-mechanical equipment at customer site * Representing the company to the customer and accountability for customer satisfaction with service * Coordinating actions with customers to minimize downtime. * Preparation of field service report on customer support activity and providing documentation to other supporting functions on re\-occurring problems * Interface with technical support, headquarter engineering, and manufacturing engineering on maintenance procedures and equipment reliability * Customer service activities associated with updating, troubleshooting, diagnosing and repairing complex electro\-optical and electro\-mechanical equipment at customer site * Representing the company to the customer and accountability for customer satisfaction with service * Coordinating actions with customers to minimize downtime. * Preparation of field service report on customer support activity and providing documentation to other supporting functions on re\-occurring problems * Interface with technical support, headquarter engineering and manufacturing engineering on maintenance procedures and equipment reliability Essential soft and technical skills: * Bachelor’s Level Degree in Electronics or Electrical Engineering or Mechanical Engineering or equivalent * Good practical skills in electronic, electrical, pneumatic, mechanical assembly * Ability to read and work with engineering drawings, schematics, and technical manuals * Excellent analytical problem\-solving skills * Good presentation and communication skills * Availability to travel to other European sites on a limited basis * Availability for training periods in USA /Asia * Proficiency in MS office tools * A valid driving license is required for the country you will be working in. As part of the Customer Service Team, the successful candidate will be required to work a days shift pattern in support of a 7 day X 24\-hour manufacturing environment. A shift cycle allowance will apply. KLA offers a challenging and multinational environment at the forefront of high technology and a market highly competitive package. Successful applicants will be formally trained at one of our award\-winning training centers in the USA California, Singapore, or Taiwan. **Minimum Qualifications** Master's Level Degree and 0 years related work experience; Bachelor's Level Degree and related work experience of 2 years *We offer a competitive, family friendly total rewards package. We design our programs to reflect our commitment to an inclusive environment, while ensuring we provide benefits that meet the diverse needs of our employees.* *KLA is proud to be an equal opportunity employer* Be aware of potentially fraudulent job postings or suspicious recruiting activity by persons that are currently posing as KLA employees. KLA never asks for any financial compensation to be considered for an interview, to become an employee, or for equipment. Further, KLA does not work with any recruiters or third parties who charge such fees either directly or on behalf of KLA. Please ensure that you have searched KLA’s Careers website for legitimate job postings. KLA follows a recruiting process that involves multiple interviews in person or on video conferencing with our hiring managers. If you are concerned that a communication, an interview, an offer of employment, or that an employee is not legitimate, please send an email to talent.acquisition@kla.com to confirm the person you are communicating with is an employee. We take your privacy very seriously and confidentially handle your information.

title: Data Analyst
description: **Data Analyst \- Private Equity Reporting \& Investment Systems** **• Join a global private equity investment business** **• Build expertise in SQL, reporting, and fund systems** **• Strong career growth through high\-impact projects** An exciting opportunity has opened for a Data Analyst to join a leading global investment management organisation. This role combines project delivery, SQL reporting, investment data analysis, and financial systems support, offering excellent exposure to private equity, fund accounting, and investment operations. You will work closely with Fund Accounting, Investment Operations, and Fund Management teams to deliver reporting solutions, support investment platforms, and drive data\-focused projects across the business. Working alongside stakeholders in Dublin and international offices, you will contribute to system enhancements, reporting improvements, data quality initiatives, and automation projects that strengthen operational efficiency and support business growth. **Why This Role Stands Out** • Strong exposure to private equity, fund accounting, and investment operations • Opportunity to lead reporting and data\-driven improvement projects • Work with business\-critical investment data, SQL reporting, and financial systems • Partner with Operations, Fund Managers, Finance, and Technology teams • Excellent long\-term career development within a global investment organisation **What You'll Be Doing** • Deliver reporting and data analysis projects across investment operations • Develop and maintain SQL reports supporting fund accounting and investment teams • Analyse private equity and investment data to support operational decision\-making • Improve data quality, reporting accuracy, and consistency across financial systems • Gather business requirements and support investment system enhancement projects • Assist with testing, documentation, reporting development, and application support • Identify opportunities to automate reporting and streamline operational processes • Work closely with Operations, Fund Accounting, and Fund Managers **What You Bring** • Experience in a Data Analyst, Reporting Analyst, Financial Systems Analyst, or similar role • Strong SQL, reporting, and data analysis experience • Experience working with financial systems **Package \& Benefits** • Up to €55,000 \+ Pension contribution • Exposure to global private equity operations, reporting projects, and investment technology Interested in a role where you can combine SQL, reporting, and project delivery within a global private equity and investment management environment? Contact Serena Akbib on \+353 1 960 9972 or send your CV to serena.akbib@archer.ie.

title: Data Analyst 2
description: Optum is a global organization that delivers care, aided by technology to help millions of people live healthier lives. Here, you will find a culture guided by diversity and inclusion, talented peers, comprehensive benefits and career development opportunities. Come make an impact on the communities we serve as you help us advance health equity on a global scale. Join us to start **Caring. Connecting. Growing together.** *Careers with Optum offer flexible work arrangements and individuals who live and work in the Republic of Ireland will have the opportunity to split their monthly work hours between our Dublin or Letterkenny offices and telecommuting from a home\-based office in a hybrid work model.* **Primary Responsibilities** * Support engineers and business users of Individual and Family Plans (IFP) PCOR reporting applications * Validate new or enhanced technical developments and work closely with engineers to achieve quality and accuracy * Data validation and testing, load process building blocks and customizations * Demonstrate data literacy and be resourceful in terms of understanding Medicare overall, STAR measures, gap closures and claims * Learn about the products, share ideas and be able to train or influence other team members You'll be rewarded and recognized for your performance in an environment that will challenge you and give you clear direction on what it takes to succeed in your role as well as provide development for other roles you may be interested in. **Required Qualifications** * Bachelor's degree or proven experience in a relevant field * Proven experience working with relational data and SQL programming (SQL Server, Teradata, or similar databases) * Proven experience in a QA role \- testing, analyzing and validating data * Proven experience in data processing involving data movement between various servers (SQL/Snowflake/Hive), working with text files, and other data sources * Experience of business intelligence solutions and architecture (Power BI, Tableau or similar) * Experience with the software development life cycle (i.e. Business Requirements, Technical Documentation, Quality Assurance) * Self\-guided in terms of understating data in physical tables or views, and ability to create/ enhance stored procedures or functions pertaining to report feeds, or any input/output * Ability to develop and manage title: Data Annotation and Quality Specialist
description: Akara Robotics are currently hiring for a Data Annotation and Quality Specialist to join their team. **About The Role:** =================== We are looking for a Data Annotation and Quality Specialist to support the development and quality assurance of Akara’s AI products. Working closely with Akara’s AI and operations teams, as well as external clinical partners, you will review, interpret, and annotate operating room data from a variety of sources, including video footage, system\-generated data streams, graphical plots, and clinical records. You will identify relevant clinical and operational events, assess the accuracy of system\-generated outputs, and ensure that annotated data consistently meets clearly defined quality standards. The role will initially focus on hands\-on data annotation, validation, and quality assurance. As Akara’s annotation function grows, you will have the opportunity to take on broader responsibilities in process improvement, training, workflow coordination, and quality management, with the potential to progress into a team leadership position. This role is well suited to someone who enjoys taking ownership, solving practical problems, and working in a fast\-moving environment where their contribution will have a clear and visible impact. **Key Responsibilities:** ------------------------- * Review and annotate operating room data, including video, sensor outputs, graphical plots, and clinical records * Identify relevant clinical and operational events using established annotation guidelines * Review system\-generated data and flag errors, inconsistencies, or uncertain cases * Perform quality checks to ensure annotations are accurate, complete, and consistent * Compare information across multiple data sources to resolve discrepancies * Maintain clear and accurate records of completed annotation work * Follow data handling, privacy, and information security procedures * Escalate ambiguous or complex cases for further review * Contribute feedback on annotation guidelines, tools, and quality assurance processes * Support the development of more efficient and scalable annotation workflows * As the team grows, assist with training, mentoring, and coordinating other annotators **What Success Looks Like:** ---------------------------- During your first 12 months, you will: * Consistently produce accurate and reliable annotations * Meet agreed productivity and quality targets * Develop a strong understanding of Akara’s annotation standards and operating room workflows * Identify recurring data quality issues and contribute to their resolution * Help improve annotation guidance, quality controls, and review processes * Support the onboarding and training of new annotation team members * Take increasing ownership of annotation projects and workflows **Key Requirements:** --------------------- * Exceptional attention to detail and the ability to complete repetitive analytical tasks with a high degree of accuracy * Ability to follow detailed instructions, guidelines, and quality standards consistently * Ability to interpret visual information and identify patterns or discrepancies across multiple data sources * Strong computer literacy and proficiency with standard software applications * Good organisational and time\-management skills * Strong written and verbal communication skills * Ability to work independently while contributing effectively within a team * Eligibility to work in Ireland without requiring visa sponsorship **Desirable Qualifications and Experience:** -------------------------------------------- * Previous experience in data annotation, data labelling, quality assurance, data review, auditing, or a similar analytical role * A bachelor’s degree or equivalent qualification in a STEM discipline, health sciences, biomedical engineering, data science, computer science, or a related field * Previous experience annotating video, image, sensor, or healthcare\-related datasets * An understanding of clinical terminology, operating room processes, or healthcare workflows * Experience using annotation, labelling, or data\-quality management tools * Experience working with confidential, sensitive, or regulated data * Previous experience training, mentoring, or coordinating the work of others * Experience supervising or managing a small team **About Akara:** ================ Akara is a spin\-out from Trinity College that helps hospitals perform more procedures and reduce infections through faster and more efficient room decontamination, powered by cutting\-edge robotic automation and AI. This is important because patients that get infections spend three times longer in hospitals, and infections acquired in hospitals lead to four times more deaths than road traffic accidents. Akara is currently supported by Enterprise Ireland through their high potential startups program (HPSU). To date they have raised €3M in funding, including a €2\.5M grant from the European Innovation Council, Europe’s most prestigious deep\-tech funding program. They are currently raising a €2\.5M seed funding round.

title: Data Center Technician
description: ****Overview**** As a Microsoft Data Center Technician (DCT), you will develop an understanding of standard processes and procedures for preparing, installing, performing diagnostics, troubleshooting, replacing, and/or decommissioning equipment under the guidance of more experienced Data Center colleagues. This opportunity will allow you to develop an understanding of our Data Center environment, familiarize yourself with security and data management procedures, and accelerate your career growth in the process. Microsoft’s Cloud Operations \& Innovation (CO\+I) is the engine that powers our cloud services. As a CO\+I DCT, you will perform a key role in delivering the core infrastructure and foundational technologies for Microsoft's online services including Bing, Office 365, Xbox, OneDrive, and the Microsoft Azure platform. As a group, CO\+I is focused on the personal and professional development of all employees and offers training and growth opportunities including Career Rotation Programs, Diversity \& Inclusion trainings and events, and professional certifications. Our infrastructure is comprised of a large global portfolio of more than 200 Data Centers in 32 countries and millions of servers. Our foundation is built upon and managed by a team of subject matter experts working to support services for more than 1 billion customers and 20 million businesses in over 90 countries worldwide. With environmental sustainability and optimization at the forefront of our data center design and operations, we continue to grow and evolve as we meet the ever\-changing business demands that hold Microsoft as a world\-class cloud provider. Microsoft’s mission is to empower every person and every organization on the planet to achieve more. As employees we come together with a growth mindset, innovate to empower others, and collaborate to realize our shared goals. Each day we build on our values of respect, integrity, and accountability to create a culture of inclusion where everyone can thrive at work and beyond. ****Responsibilities**** * Contributes to issue tracking through escalations, asking questions, and seeking guidance on timely resolution of issues from more experienced technicians. * Participates in daily safety briefings, completes required training aligned to the role and workload including safety training, complies with safety procedures (e.g., equipment use, lifting, electrical hazards, ladder/rolling stair use). * Completes assigned tickets efficiently and in alignment with Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) while meeting established Service Level Agreements (SLAs) with guidance from other technicians. * Complies with security and data management procedures and policies with guidance from other technicians and completes the required security training. * Maintains a strong client focus to understand the impact of their work when completing tickets and assigned tasks. * Contributes to a positive and effective team environment by sharing information with others, contributing to regular team meetings, asking questions, and staying apprised of the status of others' work. * Has pride and a sense of accountability for the service quality, completeness, and ownership of the data center facilities. * Embodies our culture and values. ****Qualifications**** **Required** **Qualifications** * Completed High/Secondary School, GED, an apprenticeship/vocational qualification, or equivalent experience and basic knowledge of computer hardware and components. * You may be required to travel between sites as part of this role so a valid driver’s license will be required. While not required, we also look for the following **Preferred Qualifications:** * Experience supporting IT equipment or related technology. * Applicable certifications: CompTIA (A\+, Server\+, Network\+), Basic Structure Cabling (BSC). ****Background Check Requirements:**** Ability to meet Microsoft, customer and/or government security screening requirements are required for this role. These requirements include, but are not limited to the following specialized security screenings: * Microsoft Cloud Background Check: This position will be required to pass the Microsoft Cloud background check upon hire/transfer and every two years thereafter. * As stated in the job posting, this position requires verification of citizenship or other protected status to meet legal requirements of the role. Data Center Technicians ATR\-B \- The typical base pay range for this role across Ireland is € 39,600\.00 \- € 49,100\.00 per year. Certain roles may be eligible for benefits and other compensation. **Find Additional Benefits And Pay Information Here:** https://careers.microsoft.com/v2/global/en/corporate\-pay/ireland\-corporate\-pay.html Data Center Technicians ATR\-C \- The typical base pay range for this role across Ireland is € 42,500\.00 \- € 61,000\.00 per year. Certain roles may be eligible for benefits and other compensation. **Find Additional Benefits And Pay Information Here:** https://careers.microsoft.com/v2/global/en/corporate\-pay/ireland\-corporate\-pay.html This position will be open for a minimum of 5 days, with applications accepted on an ongoing basis until the position is filled. Microsoft is an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to age, ancestry, citizenship, color, family or medical care leave, gender identity or expression, genetic information, immigration status, marital status, medical condition, national origin, physical or mental disability, political affiliation, protected veteran or military status, race, ethnicity, religion, sex (including pregnancy), sexual orientation, or any other characteristic protected by applicable local laws, regulations and ordinances. If you need assistance with religious accommodations and/or a reasonable accommodation due to a disability during the application process, read more about **requesting accommodations.**

title: Design Engineer – Transportation (Urban/Light Rail Environment)
description: **Design Engineer – Transportation (Urban/Light Rail Environment)** =================================================================== Roughan O’Donovan is looking to recruit a design engineer with three to seven years’ relevant experience for a permanent position within our transportation team **Job type:** Permanent **Location**: Sandyford, D.18 **About the role** ------------------ You will contribute to the delivery of projects focused on enhancing and upgrading transport infrastructure, with a particular emphasis on the design and coordination of interfaces with light rail systems. You will work closely with multidisciplinary teams to develop practical, high\-quality design solutions in complex urban environments. While experience in light rail projects is desirable, it is not essential, and candidates with transport design experience and an interest in integrated transport schemes are encouraged to apply. **About our transportation team** --------------------------------- Our highly skilled team has extensive experience in delivering transportation projects across Ireland and the UK. These include upgraded rail lines, light rail schemes, motorways, public transport infrastructure, rural roads and active travel projects. Our team works across the full lifecycle of transport infrastructure, from initial investigations and modelling through to detailed design, construction management and asset maintenance. Current and recent projects include: * TII Luas Construction Framework * Liffey Tolka Project * George’s Dock Bridge, Dublin * Grow College Green * BusConnects Dublin * enhancing Motorway Operations Services **Essential** ------------- * A bachelor’s degree in civil engineering (Level 8 in the National Framework of Qualifications) or equivalent * At least three years’ design or construction experience in civil engineering projects * Knowledge of relevant Irish and UK design standards (TII Publications, DMRB, DMURS, Traffic Signs, Cycle Design and Traffic Management Guidelines) * Experience in preparing reports * Experience of working with quality management systems * Proficiency in the Microsoft Office Suite (Excel, Word, Outlook) * Knowledge of BIM environments and associated workflows * Ability to work in a team environment * Motivated individual with a strong work ethic * Fluency/high level of proficiency in spoken and written English **Desirable** ------------- * A master’s degree in civil engineering (Level 9 in the National Framework of Qualifications) or equivalent * Chartered or working towards becoming a chartered member of a recognised professional body in the engineering sector * Competency in AutoCAD * Competency in 3D modelling software (Civil 3D, OpenRoads, MX) * Knowledge of traffic modelling software packages, such as Paramics, LinSig and Junctions * Experience in drainage design * Experience in pavement design * Experience in light rail projects **About Roughan O'Donovan** --------------------------- Roughan and O’Donovan (ROD) is a leading civil, environmental and structural engineering consultancy. An independently owned firm, we employ a multidisciplinary team of more than 260 people, of 20 different nationalities, in our Irish and UK offices. Our people are the heart of our business, and we invest heavily in their personal and professional development through all stages of career development. We are an Engineers Ireland CPD Accredited Employer and operate an Institution of Civil Engineers (ICE) training scheme. We have well\-established graduate and chartership support programmes. We offer **wellness packages, flexible working and life\-event supports**, so whatever challenges life may bring, you can feel secure in the knowledge that you will be taken care of. **How to apply** ---------------- * Address your cover letter to Human Resources * Apply by clicking the link: https://shorturl.at/yoZTi Roughan O’Donovan is an equal opportunity employer. title: DevOps Engineer
description: We are partnering with a leading insurance client on a large\-scale transformation programme and are seeking a DevOps / QA Engineer to support the delivery of resilient CI/CD infrastructure, automated testing, and deployment reliability across critical enterprise platforms. This role sits between DevOps, platform engineering, and quality assurance teams, with a strong focus on automation, platform resilience, and continuous improvement. While not a formal management role, this position will suit someone who enjoys taking ownership, driving standards, and acting as a technical lead across CI/CD and QA initiatives. **Key Responsibilities** * Build, optimise, and maintain robust CI/CD infrastructure and deployment pipelines * Develop and maintain automated smoke, integration, performance, and security testing within the deployment lifecycle * Design and implement automated rollback and failover procedures to minimise downtime and improve platform resilience * Partner closely with engineering teams to identify build failures and drive technical remediation activities * Support continuous improvement across deployment automation, testing standards, and release quality * Prepare and deliver weekly system demonstrations covering infrastructure health, performance benchmarks, and security scanning metrics * Contribute to best practices across DevOps, QA automation, and release engineering processes **Required Experience** * 4\+ years’ experience across DevOps, CI/CD engineering, and QA/testing environments * Strong experience with CI/CD pipelines, deployment automation, and version control workflows * Hands\-on experience with automated testing frameworks for integration and infrastructure testing * Strong scripting capability with experience writing reusable automation and verification scripts * Experience troubleshooting deployment failures and supporting remediation activities across engineering teams * Strong communication skills with the ability to work cross\-functionally across technical teams **Desirable Experience** * Experience within enterprise or regulated environments * Exposure to cloud infrastructure and Infrastructure as Code practices * Experience supporting highly available production systems **How to Apply** If you are a DevOps / QA Engineer looking for a role with strong technical ownership please apply with your latest CV.

title: Dispatch & Distribution Supervisor (Night Shift)
description: Job Purpose The Dispatch \& Distribution Supervisor is responsible for overseeing and managing all night shift warehouse, dispatch, and distribution operations. This role ensures the efficient picking, packing, loading, and dispatch of customer orders while supervising warehouse staff and drivers to ensure all deliveries are prepared accurately and leave on schedule. Key Responsibilities * Supervise and coordinate all night shift warehouse, dispatch, and distribution activities. * Manage all picking and packing operations to ensure customer orders are completed accurately and on time. * Oversee the loading and dispatch process to ensure goods are prepared and delivered according to schedules. * Supervise warehouse staff and delivery drivers throughout the shift. * Ensure drivers report to work on time and manage attendance issues where necessary. * Ensure drivers load the correct goods onto their vehicles before departure. * Verify that all delivery paperwork and vehicle loading requirements are completed accurately. * Coordinate loading schedules and dispatch activities to maximise operational efficiency. * Monitor delivery schedules and ensure vehicles leave on time. * Allocate work effectively and monitor team performance against operational targets. * Investigate and report stock discrepancies, damaged goods, shortages, or delivery issues. * Ensure effective communication between warehouse staff, drivers, and management. * Conduct shift handovers and provide operational updates and reports. * Maintain high standards of housekeeping, organisation, and health and safety compliance. * Support staff training and promote a positive and productive working environment. * Resolve operational issues efficiently during the night shift. Skills \& Experience Required * Previous supervisory experience within logistics, warehouse, dispatch, or distribution operations. * Experience managing dispatch activities, driver coordination, and warehouse operations. * Strong leadership and people management skills. * Excellent organisational and time management abilities. * Strong attention to detail and ability to work under pressure. * Good communication and problem\-solving skills. * Experience using warehouse or transport management systems would be beneficial. * Competent computer skills. Personal Attributes * Reliable and dependable * Strong leadership skills * Proactive and self\-motivated * Strong attention to detail * Flexible and adaptable * Positive attitude and strong work ethic Pay: From €38,000\.00 per year Work Location: In person

title: Dublin Secure Room Clerk
description: Are you looking for a career move that will put you at the heart of a global financial institution? Then bring your skills in analysis, problem solving and communication to Issuer Services Operation team. Shape your career with Citi in Dublin. By joining Citi, you will become part of a global organisation whose mission is to serve as a trusted partner to our clients by responsibly providing financial services that enable growth and economic progress. Citi has had a presence in Ireland since 1965, it was one of the first foreign banks to open an office in the country and is the Citibank Europe Plc Headquarters. **Team/Role Overview** We are seeking a highly responsible individual to join our Dublin Secure Room within Issuer Services Operation. The objective of this role is to assist in the physical safekeeping , reconciliation and destruction of sensitive financial instruments. The role involves the secure handling of short and medium term debt including Certificate of Deposits, European Commercial Paper, Bonds, European Medium Term Notes and Warrants in compliance with internal controls and industry regulations. The ideal candidate will have a strong focus on accuracy and operational efficiency within a highly secure and controlled environment. **What You’ll Do** * Safekeeping – Print, Authenticate and File physical certificates . Ensure accuracy and completeness of all associated documentation * Destruction – Complete Destruction process on a daily basis. Following termination of an Issuers debt obligations (either at expected maturity date or an earlier date if repurchased) the Global Note representing the debt must be destroyed. A report of items ready for destruction is run each day showing all items due to be destroyed following receipt of Disposal Authority from the ICSDs. * Reconciliation – Perform a reconciliation of the physical records against the internal books and records. This is part of the Client Asset Regulatory (CAR) Requirement. On a Bi\-Annual basis the Dublin Secure Room is required to complete a full count of all securities that are held as Common Depository on behalf of the International Centralized Securities Depository (ICSD). The details of which are entered in a system under dual control. * Operational Excellence \& Process Contribution \- Complete operational tasks focused on administering defined procedures, analyses and report preparation * Risk Mitigation \& Control Adherence \-Minimize risk to the bank by monitoring errors and suggesting solutions that reduce errors, and ensure adherence to audit and control policies * Policy and Procedure Enhancement \- Identify policy gaps and propose efforts to streamline the securities and derivatives settlement processes * Data Analysis \& Reporting \-Monitor, analyse and evaluate processes and data to ensure the accuracy and quality of completed settlement tasks * Regulatory \& Ethical Stewardship \- Appropriately assess risk when business decisions are made, demonstrating particular consideration for the firm's reputation and safeguarding Citigroup, its clients and assets, by driving compliance with applicable laws, rules and regulations, adhering to Policy, applying sound ethical judgment regarding personal behaviour, conduct and business practices, and escalating, managing and reporting control issues with transparency. **What We’ll Need From You** * Relevant experience * Fundamental knowledge of securities activities, policies, procedures, and concepts and advantage * Consistently demonstrates clear and concise written and verbal communication skills * Bachelor’s Degree/University degree or equivalent experience in Business, Accounting or Finance **What We Can Offer You** By joining Citi Dublin, you will receive a competitive base salary (which is annually reviewed) and enjoy a whole host of additional benefits that support you (and your family) to be well, live well and save well. Alongside these benefits Citi is committed to ensuring our workplace is where everyone feels comfortable coming to work as their whole self every day. We want the best talent around the world to be energised to join us, motivated to stay, and empowered to thrive. **Sounds like Citi has everything you need? Then apply to discover the true extent of your capabilities.** **Additional Notes :** * This role requires 100% on\-site presence within the Dublin Secure Room facility. * Candidates must be able to commit to a full\-time, in\-person work schedule. * This job description provides a high\-level review of the types of work performed. Other job\-related duties may be assigned as required. \-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\- **Job Family Group:** Operations \- Transaction Services \-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\- **Job Family:** Securities and Derivatives Processing \-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\- **Time Type:** Full time \-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\- **Primary Location Full Time Salary Range:** €35 950,00 \- €46 750,00 \-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\- **Most Relevant Skills** Please see the requirements listed above. \-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\- **Other Relevant Skills** For complementary skills, please see above and/or contact the recruiter. \-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\- *Citi is an equal opportunity employer, and qualified candidates will receive consideration without regard to their race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability, status as a protected veteran, or any other characteristic protected by law.* *If you are a person with a disability and need a reasonable accommodation to use our search tools and/or apply for a career opportunity review* *Accessibility at Citi.* *View Citi’s EEO Policy Statement and the Know Your Rights poster.*

title: Electrical Engineer
description: **The Opportunity** **THE OPPORTUNITY** At Stantec Ireland, you’ll join a growing Energy team at the forefront of a transforming transmission and distribution sector. With rising demand and ambitious decarbonisation targets across Ireland , this is an opportunity to contribute to critical infrastructure shaping the energy transition. As an Electrical Engineer specialising in HV cable design, you’ll work on a diverse range of projects including HVDC interconnectors, offshore and onshore wind, substations, and grid connections—supporting initiatives aligned with EirGrid ’s network development. You’ll collaborate within a multidisciplinary team across all project stages, from FEED to delivery, contributing technical expertise while developing your career in a supportive, innovation\-driven environment. **About The Role** ou will take on a key role within our growing Energy team, supporting the design and delivery of high\-voltage cable systems across a range of major infrastructure and renewable energy projects throughout Ireland . Working as part of a collaborative, multidisciplinary environment, you will contribute to projects spanning HVDC interconnectors, offshore and onshore wind farms, substations, and grid connection schemes. Your responsibilities will include supporting and leading design activities across all project phases—from feasibility and FEED through to detailed design and delivery—ensuring high\-quality, efficient, and innovative engineering solutions. You will work closely with internal teams and external stakeholders, including key clients such as EirGrid , helping to address complex technical challenges and deliver infrastructure that underpins Ireland’s transition to a more sustainable energy future. This role offers the opportunity to apply your technical expertise, mentor junior engineers, and play an active part in shaping impactful projects, while continuing to develop your career within a supportive and forward\-looking organisation. **YOUR IMPACT** In this role, you will help deliver safe, reliable and sustainable water infrastructure for communities across Ireland. Through your expertise in process design, you will develop effective treatment solutions that improve water quality and support resilient systems for the future. Working closely with multidisciplinary teams and clients, you will contribute to innovative, practical solutions across the full project lifecycle. Your work will help ensure designs are efficient, compliant and deliver long\-term value while supporting the collaborative culture that defines our team at Stantec. **About You** You are a proactive and technically capable Electrical Engineer with a strong understanding of the electricity transmission and distribution sector. You thrive in collaborative, multidisciplinary teams and are confident coaching and supporting colleagues across the wider team. You will ideally hold a Bachelor's or Master's degree in Electrical Engineering (or a related field) from an accredited institution, with membership of Institution of Civil Engineers (EI) is preferred. You bring experience in civil substation engineering and a broad awareness of HV civil engineering projects. You have hands\-on experience in the design of transmission and distribution systems across all stages (Concept, FEED, and Detail Design), applying international standards for utility clients. You can coordinate effectively across disciplines, resolving design issues and ensuring practical, innovative solutions. **Your Technical Skills Include** * Cable installation design, including routing layouts, trench/trenchless sections, bonding and phasing, pulling, termination, and jointing arrangements. * Proficiency with CYMCAP software for cable sizing calculations to international standards. * Strong computer literacy in MS Office (Excel, Word, PowerPoint, Outlook) and design tools such as AutoCAD or MicroStation. You are adaptable and capable of managing challenging workloads under changing priorities, delivering high\-quality results in a dynamic environment. **WHY JOIN US** * We put people first: We're proud of our friendly, inclusive and collaborative environment where everyone feels valued and driven to succeed. * We are driven to achieve: Named in the top 50 companies to work by Glassdoor 2025\. Recognised by Corporate Knights as one of the world's top 10 most sustainable companies 2025\. \= * We do what is right: Industry leading training and development as well as paid for professional subscriptions. A professional progression plan, supporting you to become chartered. * We are better together: A supportive and collaborative team environment to assist with the growth of your career. * Growth: We're on an exciting growth journey across the Ireland \- we want you to be part of it! * Great Benefits: Competitive salary, enhanced pension plan, annual leave with the option to buy/sell additional days, private medical insurance, group income protection \& life assurance included as standard! **About Stantec** Whether you’re starting out on your engineering career or are looking for a fresh challenge, Ryan Hanley, now part of Stantec, is a great career move. Based in Dublin, Galway, Castlebar and Cork, we are actively recruiting for team players with experience in any of the following areas – water and wastewater treatment process and network design, integrated network management, structural design, site development, road and bridge engineering, project management and administration. You’ll be working for a dynamic company with an excellent reputation in the industry. Ryan Hanley, now part of Stantec, offers a great working environment amongst friendly professionals with an emphasis on teamwork. It’s a company where your ideas and opinions will be valued and your efforts recognised and rewarded. We have been awarded the silver Investors in Diversity accreditation by the Irish Centre for Diversity for our commitment to quality, diversity and inclusion (EDI) in the workplace.

title: Electrical Technician
description: JLL supports the Whole You, personally and professionally. Our people at JLL are shaping the future of real estate for a better world by combining world class services, advisory and technology to our clients. We are committed to hiring the best, most talented people in our industry; and we support them through professional growth, flexibility, and personalized benefits to manage life in and outside of work. Whether you’ve got deep experience in commercial real estate, skilled trades, and technology, or you’re looking to apply your relevant experience to a new industry, we empower you to shape a brighter way forward so you can thrive professionally and personally. Electrical TechnicianRole Overview We are seeking a qualified Electrical Technician to join our facilities management team in Ireland. The successful candidate will be responsible for the installation, maintenance, repair, and testing of electrical systems and equipment across commercial properties, ensuring compliance with Irish electrical regulations and safety standards. Key Responsibilities The Electrical Technician will carry out planned preventative maintenance and reactive repairs on electrical systems including lighting, power distribution, emergency lighting, fire alarms, and building management systems. You will respond to electrical faults and breakdowns, diagnose problems efficiently, and implement effective solutions to minimize downtime. Conducting electrical safety testing and inspections in accordance with ETCI National Rules for Electrical Installations and maintaining accurate records of all work completed form essential parts of the role. You will ensure all electrical work complies with relevant Irish legislation, health and safety requirements, and building codes. Collaborating with other trades and contractors on site, you will support project work and minor installations as required. The role includes maintaining electrical equipment inventories, ordering parts and materials, and providing technical guidance to junior staff members when needed. Required Qualifications and ExperienceCandidates must hold a recognized electrical qualification such as Advanced Certificate Craft \- Electrical or equivalent Level 6 qualification on the NFQ. Current Safe Electric Registration (RECI or ECSSA) is essential. A minimum of 3\-5 years' post\-qualification experience in commercial or facilities maintenance environments is required, along with comprehensive knowledge of Irish electrical regulations, ETCI wiring rules, and health and safety legislation. Strong diagnostic and problem\-solving abilities are necessary, with the ability to work independently and as part of a team. Excellent communication skills and customer service orientation are important, as is proficiency in using computerized maintenance management systems Desirable Skills Experience with building management systems (BMS), HVAC controls, and access control systems would be advantageous. Knowledge of energy management systems and sustainability initiatives is beneficial. Previous experience in corporate office environments or large commercial properties is valued, as are relevant health and safety certifications including manual handling and working at heights. What We Offer We provide competitive salary packages commensurate with experience, along with comprehensive benefits including pension schemes, health insurance options, and ongoing professional development opportunities. You will work within a supportive team environment with opportunities for career progression and exposure to diverse commercial real estate projects across Ireland. If this job description resonates with you, we encourage you to apply even if you don’t meet all of the requirements below. We’re interested in getting to know you and what you bring to the table! Personalized benefits that support personal well\-being and growth: JLL recognizes the impact that the workplace can have on your wellness, so we offer a supportive culture and comprehensive benefits package that prioritizes mental, physical and emotional health. About JLL – We’re JLL—a leading professional services and investment management firm specializing in real estate. We have operations in over 80 countries and a workforce of over 102,000 individuals around the world who help real estate owners, occupiers and investors achieve their business ambitions. As a global Fortune 500 company, we also have an inherent responsibility to drive sustainability and corporate social responsibility. That’s why we’re committed to our purpose to shape the future of real estate for a better world. We’re using the most advanced technology to create rewarding opportunities, amazing spaces and sustainable real estate solutions for our clients, our people, and our communities. Our core values of teamwork, ethics and excellence are also fundamental to everything we do and we’re honored to be recognized with awards for our success by organizations both globally and locally. Creating a diverse and inclusive culture where we all feel welcomed, valued and empowered to achieve our full potential is important to who we are today and where we’re headed in the future. And we know that unique backgrounds, experiences and perspectives help us think bigger, spark innovation and succeed together.

title: Equity Analyst

title: Escalation Engineer, Alpha (Remote) - $60,000/year USD
description: A student attempts to begin a lesson, but the application fails to load. A guide's interface displays incorrect mastery data. Both L1 support and the AI have already attempted resolution without success. The issue now falls to you — and a child's educational session depends on your ability to identify the underlying cause. The majority of your time will be spent on tickets that others were unable to resolve, spanning the multiple applications that comprise Alpha's learning platform. You will replicate the issue in an environment matching the student's or guide's configuration, analyze logs and documentation, leverage AI to accelerate your workflow, then validate all outputs — because an incorrect solution deployed in a classroom setting escalates rapidly. Ramping up on a new product nearly every week is standard. This is the core of the role, not an exception. You assume full ownership of each issue: replicate it, identify the root cause, and resolve it with the minimum number of interactions necessary to restore functionality for the student or guide. The diagnostic reasoning you document strengthens both the next support agent and future AI workflows, ensuring your solution continues to deliver value well beyond the current ticket. As you progress, you will become the escalation point for the team's most challenging education\-product issues. If you are drawn to difficult, ambiguous challenges, and you genuinely care that the product ultimately supports a child's learning, submit your application. **What You Will Be Doing** * Resolve complex, escalated support tickets from students, parents, and guides that neither AI nor L1 support could successfully close. * Replicate issues in an environment that accurately reflects the user's device, application version, and configuration, using logs and actual user artifacts. * Conduct thorough investigation across Alpha's learning applications (ticketing systems, Slack channels, knowledge repositories, logs) prior to escalation. * Utilize AI tools (including ChatGPT or Claude) to accelerate diagnostic work, anchor them in verified documentation, and validate all generated outputs. * Communicate with clarity and composure to non\-technical users, collecting sufficient information upfront to enable single\-pass resolution. * Escalate issues to engineering with comprehensive diagnostic context when a genuine product defect is identified. * Create detailed documentation of your diagnostic process so subsequent agents and AI workflows can leverage it. **What You Won’t Be Doing** * Working from scripts or decision trees for a single product. * Processing the simple ticket queue. AI already resolves issues that require only knowledge\-base lookups. * Escalating difficult problems to engineering without first replicating and narrowing the scope. * Allowing AI to perform reasoning on your behalf, or forwarding its output without verification. * Waiting for direction or for others to remove blockers from your path. **Escalation Engineer Key Responsibilities** Diagnose and resolve complex, ambiguous customer issues to root cause across Alpha's suite of education products. **Basic Requirements** * 2\+ years of hands\-on technical experience in roles such as technical support, software engineering, QA, system administration, or DevOps. The specific job title need not be "support." * Proficient in making and interpreting REST API calls and JSON, understanding HTTP status codes (such as 401 vs 404 and 429 vs 403\), and working within command\-line environments and log files. * Practical experience incorporating AI tools (such as ChatGPT or Claude) into your daily technical workflows. * Professional\-level fluency in written and spoken English. * Available to work a full 40\-hour week during US business hours (Eastern Time). This position operates on a full US\-daytime schedule. * Located in North America, Central America, South America, Australia, New Zealand, the United Kingdom, Ireland, or the Philippines. **Nice\-to\-have Requirements** * Background supporting non\-technical end users (within education, edtech, or consumer applications), rather than solely IT professionals. * Demonstrated experience directing AI tools and identifying their errors. * Proven ability troubleshooting across multiple unrelated products, not limited to a single platform. * Developer\-level technical depth: capable of reading code or tracing an API call when the situation requires it. **About Alpha** **Want to join a learning rebellion that’s transforming the traditional classroom?** Alpha School is on a mission to reshape education with a bold approach that harnesses AI to accelerate learning and unleash student potential. **They don’t play by the old rules.** Alpha is creating a new paradigm where students master core academics in just two hours a day, freeing up time for hands\-on, passion\-driven learning that truly prepares them for the real world. **Alpha School is rewriting the rules of education.** **Sounds too good to be true? It’s not.** There is so much to cover for this exciting role, and space here is limited. Hit the Apply button if you found this interesting and want to learn more. We look forward to meeting you! **Working with us** This is a full\-time (40 hours per week), long\-term position. The position is immediately available and requires entering into an independent contractor agreement with Crossover as a Contractor of Record. The compensation level for this role is $30 USD/hour, which equates to $60,000 USD/year assuming 40 hours per week and 50 weeks per year. The payment period is weekly. Consult www.crossover.com/help\-and\-faqs for more details on this topic. Crossover Job Code: LJ\-5729\-IE\-Dublin\-EscalationEngi

This opportunity would suit an established Supervisor, Assistant Manager or Senior Associate looking to take the next step in their career and gain broader leadership, client relationship and operational responsibility. **Key responsibilities include:** * Managing a portfolio of fund clients and acting as a key point of contact * Overseeing the production and review of NAV calculations * Reviewing trade, cash, income and expense processing activities * Performing cash and position reconciliations * Supporting the preparation of financial statements and year\-end audit deliverables * Building relationships with investment managers, custodians, brokers and other stakeholders * Assisting with team oversight, mentoring and development **Requirements:** * Approximately 4\+ years' experience within fund accounting * Experience reviewing NAVs and fund accounting processes * Previous experience mentoring, supervising or reviewing the work of others is advantageous * Financial reporting experience would be beneficial The role offers exposure to a diverse client base, excellent career progression opportunities and the chance to step into a leadership position within a well\-established international organisation. Interested or know someone who might be ? You can email me on alex.obrien@recruiters.ie or call me on 0035315225353 **By applying to this job, your CV will be assessed by a consultant for the position described, along with considering you for other relevant­ jobs as they arise in accordance with your interest, experience and skillset. Your CV may be added to our secure applicant tracking system however your details will never be shared to any other third party without your consent. You can browse a full spectrum of job opportunities and exercise your data subject rights anytime.**

title: General Operative
description: Production **Job Purpose** To support the business in achieving its daily targets **Reports To** Shift Supervisor **Key Responsibilities** Responsibilities associated with this role include, but are not limited to: * Meet daily production targets on output, waste, yield, efficiency, start\-up time by working as part of a team * Accurately record all production data on the relevant sheets provided at set time frequencies (such as downtime, waste, process sheets, non\-conformances etc.) * Ensure that all quality tests are completed as per protocols and all traceability details are carried out in line with systems requirements * Control and responsibility of all items within the area such as production tools, probes, machine parts in line with metal procedures and other such foreign matter procedures in place. Replacing when required and reporting of items if misplaced * Carry out all tasks to ensure that food safety requirements are achieved * Maintain the work area and machines in a tidy / safe / hygienic manner * Operate all equipment in accordance with training provided and approved SOP * Follow all hygiene and safety procedures * Offer suggestions / new initiatives to a changing / developing process * Work within the quality standards as set out by the business * Ensure all products are produced and packed to company / customer specification and on the ‘get it right first time’ motto * Inform line manager of any maintenance issues identified * Communicate with other members of staff to ensure continuous supply of materials to the production line to ensure uninterrupted stoppages to the process * Be flexible to ensure all orders are met, e.g., in the event of a breakdown occurring on the line **Skills / Qualifications / Experience** * Good interpersonal and communication skills * Excellent understanding of food safety (HACCP) and Health \& Safety processes * High level of accuracy and attention to detail * At least 6 months in a FMCG environment * Ability to work in a team environment * Shows flexibility in all aspects of the role * Understands the business and what needs to get done to help the business to be successful * Is proactive and thinks ahead * Excellent written and verbal English Note: As with all positions, due to the dynamic nature of Arrow Group business, key responsibilities will evolve and change over time. Some travel will be required. Appointment to this role is subject to the candidate’s eligibility to work in Ireland Apply Now

title: General Operative - Nightshift
description: * Pack out \& lining orders, bulk retrieval, order processing, fridge \& general assembly, order scanning, putting away stock, completion of van sheets –In line with Uniphar Standard operating procedures. * Unload pallets from Dublin truck using forklift, confirm quantities \& line in run sequence. * Work on the IWACS system * Work with the drivers EPOD system and have working knowledge of same. * Unload pallets for goods inwards when required. * Liaise with Citywest warehouse if required. * Process empty totes / lids for return to Citywest. * Record any issues in the hand over book for shift supervisor/manager * Work with the Supervisor and Line Leader to process Bulk Stock and/or assemble customer orders in an efficient and accurate manner **Key Skills \& Experiences** * Can do attitude. * Very good verbal and written communication skills. * Ability to work on own initiative and as part of a team. * High level of attention to detail. * Strong Customer Focus. * Respect, collaborative approach. * Highlight operational improvements to enhance service and productivity. * Basic Computer skills **Uniphar – Who we are** With a workforce of more than 3000 employees spread across Ireland, United Kingdom, Mainland Europe, MENA, and the USA, Uniphar is a trusted global partner to pharma and MedTech manufacturers, working to improve patient access to medicines around the world. Uniphar provides outsourced and specialized services to its clients, leveraging the strong relationships with 200\+ of the world’s best known pharmaco\-medical manufacturers across multiple geographies, enabled by our cutting\-edge digital technology and our highly expert teams. Uniphar is organised into three key divisions: Supply Chain Retail \& On Demand, Uniphar Med Tech and Uniphar Pharma. **Supply Chain \& Retail** Supply Chain Services provides both pre\-wholesale and wholesale distribution of pharmaceutical, healthcare and animal health products to pharmacies and hospital nationwide. The business supports a diverse customer base through the provision of strong service levels coupled with innovative commercial initiatives. In addition, the business provides services and supports that help independent community pharmacy to compete more effectively in an increasingly difficult environment and runs a network of Uniphar\-owned pharmacies under the Life, Allcare, McCauleys and Hickeys symbol brands. **Pharma – On Demand** The On\-Demand business is a specialist medical supply business that sources and distributes pharmaceuticals, medical equipment, and consumable supplies to healthcare professionals in over 160 countries worldwide. We have offices in Ireland, UK, Europe, USA and New Zealand. The company’s main divisions encompass everything from International Sales through Sales to NGO’s and Charities, Clinical Trials Supply, Imported (Unlicensed Medicines), Sexual Health Supplies, and the storage and distribution of medical products and devices for third parties**.** Click here to find out more: About Us \- Who we are \| Uniphar **Culture at Uniphar** Uniphar is a combination of many businesses each with its own identity, culture, values, and ways of working. Our overarching culture narrative is inclusive of each of these stores. Uniphar lives and breathes four key pillars which unpin and support our culture. We have a people first approach \- we do the right thing. We support entrepreneurial spirit where adaptability, commitment \& resilience is embodied in our way of working. We have a Common Purpose that connects our diversified businesses and people. Finally, Trust is at the heart of how we operate. Everything Uniphar does is enabled by our people and as we continue to grow domestically and internationally, we become more diverse. This diversity fuels our business and culture. **Sustainability** Sustainability is at the core of what we do and is deeply embedded in our business strategy. We want to contribute positively to the people and the world around us. Running our business in a sustainable way ensures that Uniphar will continue to prosper in the long\-term. We are focused on ensuring that each of the five pillars of our sustainability strategy are a fundamental part of our decision\-making process.

title: General Operative / B Driver - Traffic Management (Blanchardstown)
description: Interested in a career in traffic management? If you believe you have the skills and experience required for this role, please review the details below and click the **Apply button** to apply. **Traffic Management Operative \- B driver** GKTM is a traffic management company providing traffic management solutions nationwide, having operated at the forefront of the industry over the past decade, working on many large infrastructure and transport projects. **Role** We currently have a number of vacancies for Traffic Management Operatives B driver to be based primarily in our Blanchardstown Depot. As a **Traffic Management Operative \- Driver**, you will play a vital role in supporting the safe and efficient movement of traffic around roadworks, maintenance projects, and special events. Your primary responsibility will be driving company vehicles to transport traffic management equipment, materials, and personnel to and from work sites. You will also assist with the safe loading, unloading, installation, maintenance, and removal of traffic control measures, including signs, cones, barriers, and temporary traffic management systems. You will ensure all work is carried out in accordance with company procedures, traffic management plans, and health and safety regulations. The role requires a strong commitment to safety, excellent driving skills, reliability, and the ability to work effectively as part of a team. You will also interact professionally with clients, contractors, and members of the public while representing the company on site and on the road. **Training** will be delivered upon commencement to ensure the successful candidate understands how to carry out their duties safely while on site. The successful candidate will look forward to the following: **Benefits** * Competitive rates of pay * Opportunities to progress within company * Company organised events * In depth traffic management training, both in\-house and externally * Full\-time, permanent positions are available * Overtime available **Requirements** * Valid Safe Pass * Manual Handling * Full B driver license * Own transport essential for shifts which may begin outside of the hours of public transport **Responsibilities:** * **Traffic Management Equipment Setup:** Safely install and set up traffic management equipment, signs, cones, barriers, and other equipment according to established plans and procedures under the guidance of the site supervisor. * **Traffic Management:** Monitor traffic flow and respond to changing conditions, adjusting traffic control measures as necessary to ensure the safety of both workers and the public. * **Driving Company Vehicles:** Operate company vehicles, such as trucks or vans, to transport traffic management equipment and personnel to and from work sites. Drive vehicles with caution, obeying all traffic laws and company policies, and ensure the safe loading and securing of equipment. * **Safety Compliance:** Strictly adhere to all safety regulations, policies, and guidelines while carrying out duties, including wearing appropriate personal protective equipment (PPE). * **Equipment Maintenance:** Perform routine checks and maintenance of traffic management equipment to ensure proper functionality, reporting any issues to the supervisor. * **Communication:** Effectively communicate with colleagues, supervisors, and the public, providing clear and courteous instructions and information regarding traffic control measures and diversions. * **Emergency Response:** Act promptly and effectively in case of emergencies or incidents, following established procedures to mitigate risks and ensure public safety. * **Documentation:** Maintain accurate records and documentation related to traffic management activities, including equipment usage and maintenance reports. * **Environmental Awareness:** Consider and implement environmentally friendly practices in traffic management, including waste management and pollution control. * **Continuous Improvement:** Stay updated on industry best practices and participate in training programs to improve skills and knowledge related to traffic management. **Profile of the ideal candidate:** * Previous experience in traffic management or a related field is preferred but not essential. * Flexible, willing to work shifts during the day or night in line with the requirements of our clients. * Punctual. * Willing to work down the country for up to five consecutive days. * React positively to feedback provided by your mentor and other leaders within the organisation. * Strong attention to detail and the ability to work efficiently and safely in a fast\-paced environment. * Excellent communication and teamwork skills. * Physical fitness and the ability to lift heavy equipment and work outdoors in various weather conditions. Job Types: Full\-time, Permanent Pay: From €14\.50 per hour Benefits: * On\-site parking Experience: * General Operative: 1 year (required) Licence/Certification: * own transport for early commute (required) * Manual Handling (required) * Safe Pass (required) * Full B Driving Licence (required) Work authorisation: * Ireland (required) Work Location: In person
