title: General Operative from €18p/h Depending on Experience
description: **Pay Rate:** €18\.00 per hour (depending on experience) At Robinson, we specialise in the manufacture of HGV trailers, cranes, tippers, and truck bodies. We are expanding our team and are seeking an experienced and motivated **General Operative / Assembly Technician** to join our manufacturing facility. **Location:** Portnahinch, Portarlington, Co. Laois, Ireland (R32 CD40\) A well\-located facility offering a relaxed working environment away from heavy traffic and urban congestion. **Role Overview:** As an Assembly Technician, you will play a key role in the production and assembly of high\-quality trailers and related equipment. Working within a skilled team, you will be responsible for assembling components to a high standard while adhering to safety and quality requirements. **Key Responsibilities:** * Assembling HGV trailers using pre\-manufactured parts and pre\-made looms * Working with hydraulic and electrical systems, including: * Hydraulic plumbing using nylon hose * Hydraulic and electrical wiring * Installation of pre\-assembled wiring looms * Reading and interpreting hydraulic and electrical diagrams * Carrying out hydraulic and electrical troubleshooting as required * Working independently on assigned tasks while contributing as part of a team * Maintaining a tidy, organised, and safe work area * Adhering to all Health \& Safety policies, procedures, and regulations * Ensuring all work meets Robinson's quality and workmanship standards **Skills \& Experience:** * Experience or strong interest in cars, machinery, hydraulics, or mechanical assembly * Background in agricultural machinery, plant machinery, or heavy equipment manufacturing (desirable) * Good knowledge of hydraulic systems and components * Experience with hydraulic plumbing and wiring * Ability to read and understand hydraulic and electrical schematics * Strong troubleshooting and problem\-solving skills * Reliable, punctual, and well organised **Progression \& Development:** Robinson is committed to developing its people and offers clear progression opportunities, including: * Progression to Senior Assembly Technician with increased technical responsibility * Opportunity to specialise in hydraulics, electrical systems, or final build/quality control * Training and upskilling in advanced hydraulic and electrical diagnostics * Potential progression into Team Lead or Production Supervisor roles * Long\-term career development within a growing manufacturing business **What We Offer:** * Competitive hourly rate of €18\.00 (depending on experience) * Sign\-on bonus (terms apply) * Stable, long\-term employment in a growing company * Opportunities for training, development, and career progression * Supportive team environment and modern manufacturing facility

title: Graduate Energy Consultant
description: We are currently seeking applications for trainee energy consultants. This would suit any graduate in the built environment in fields such as : * Building Surveying, * Architectural Technology, * Mechanical Engineering, * Construction Management, * Building Services Engineering etc. All training will be provided and paid for. The candidate will start off as a domestic BER assessor in an environment where the necessary experience will be gained to develop into an experienced energy consultant. This could be an exciting new career as a professional in a rapidly growing industry. The role of an energy consultant typically involves three days in the office and two days surveying properties. The successful candidate will be focused on providing technical specifications and advice for customers, most of whom are contractors carrying out energy retrofit projects. Our office is based in Tullyallen Village, Co. Louth. In addition we do not operate on a rigid schedule for office days and use flexible working hours i.e. start early and early finish or vice versa. There is full use of a company vehicle at all times and so no commuting costs. **Requirements:** * The entry requirements to become a registered BER assessor are either: * A) Minimum QQI Level 6 in a construction related qualification, * B) Minimum QQI Level 6 in a non construction related qualification, but with at least two years relevant construction experience, * Ideally candidates would have a qualification in building surveying, construction studies, engineering, architectural technology, project management, civil engineering or similar, however, this is not a requirement and all candidates will be considered, * Full clean driving license. **Essential Skills**: * Possess good communication skills, * Customer focused, * Ability to work on your own initiative The successful candidate will have the chance to work alongside a team of experienced retrofit professionals \& gain knowledge of domestic and commercial energy ratings, building regulations, air tightness, ventilation validation and energy retrofits of dwellings. A generous package is available for the right candidate. For more information see below links to our websites: www.ventilationvalidation.ie www.necber.ie Job Types: Full\-time, Permanent Pay: €31,200\.00\-€35,000\.00 per year Licence/Certification: * full driving licence (required) Work authorisation: * Ireland (required) Work Location: In person

title: Graduate Engineer - Engines-EN
description: Do You Want To Progress Your Career With The Global Leader In Aviation? Under The Guidance Of Senior Engineers, Our Graduate Engineers Will Be Given The Opportunity To Gain Hands\-on Experience Across Our Technical Support Services Department Which Includes The Engine Management Technical Support Services Department specializes in managing powerplants for our clients, including engine phase in and phase out, shop visit oversight, monitoring engine condition, records review and conducting maintenance forecasts. As a Powerplant Graduate Engineer, you'll gain experience providing support to our clients, with involvement in a large variance of projects ensuring that there is never a dull moment for our graduate engineers. On completion of the programme, you’ll be the type of person who has the proven ability to solve problems with sound judgment and have effective communication skills as well as excellent organizational skills and computer skills. As part of the programme, you will be mentored by senior engineers, have the opportunity to rotate across the different engineering services departments and be involved in new and existing projects. All the above will provide you with the best training in the industry, while following a clear and structured development path. **Education Required** Applicants will have recently completed or are in the final year of a Technical Degree (B.Eng, B.Sc etc.) **The Package** We offer a competitive salary as well as a range of benefits. **Next Steps** This is an excellent opportunity to join our Engineering graduate programme and to join a multi\-award\-winning company, which is the global leader in its field. **About CAE** *At CAE, our mission is clear: to help make the world a safer place. For nearly 80 years, we’ve driven innovation in simulation, training, and mission readiness to support critical operations worldwide. By leveraging advanced technologies, we empower our customers to operate smarter, faster, and more sustainably. Join a purpose\-driven organization where bold ideas are encouraged, collaboration drives progress, and your growth fuels our shared success.* **Position Type** Regular **Equal Opportunity \& Accommodations** *CAE is committed to providing equal opportunities to all applicants, regardless of race, nationality, color, religion, sex, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, disability, neurodiversity, veteran status, age, or other characteristics protected by law. We encourage applicants who may not meet every qualification to apply. Reasonable accommodations are available—contact your recruiter or email* *CAECarrieres\-Careers@cae.com* *if needed.* **Data Privacy** Privacy Statement \| CAE *As part of our process, we may use AI‑supported tools to help review applications, with human decision‑making at every step. CAE thanks all applicants for their interest. However, only those whose background and experience match the requirements of the role will be contacted.*

title: Graduate Engineer - Water
description: **The Vacancy** **About The Company** At Nicholas O'Dwyer, sustainability is at the core of our work. If you are looking to advance your career in a socially responsible company, which has a proven track record over 90 years, and is committed to creating a sustainable planet, we invite you to be part of our team. Our mission is to provide innovative and reliable solutions using cutting edge technology and expertise, to ensure project success and employee satisfaction. We deliver infrastructure projects in water, wastewater, transportation, civil and structural engineering, flood protection, and energy including renewables. With headquarters in Ireland, we also operate in the UK, Europe, the Middle East, and Africa. As part of the RSK Group, an amalgamation of over 200 companies with more than 17,000 employees, we benefit from a global network of opportunities while retaining our brand and autonomy. **About The Role** We are seeking a motivated and enthusiastic **Graduate Engineer** to join our Water Engineering Team. This is an exciting opportunity for a recent graduate to kick\-start their career in civil or environmental engineering, working on a diverse range of water infrastructure projects. As part of the team, you will gain invaluable hands\-on experience, develop your technical and project management skills, and work on projects that positively impact communities and the environment. You will have the opportunity to contribute to all phases of water engineering projects, from feasibility studies to detailed design and construction, while learning from experienced engineers in a collaborative and supportive environment. You will structured training and mentoring throughout your first year. In addition to office\-based learning and development, you will have practical exposure through site visits and involvement in site\-based activities. **Key Duties And Responsibilities** * Building your knowledge of water engineering principles, regulations, and best practices. * Preparation of Feasibility, Route, and Site Selection Reports. * Contributing to the preparation of Concept and Detailed Design Reports, including drawings, calculations, and specifications. * Clean water network modelling with various modelling software in design and analysis. * Contributing to the preparation of Technical Specifications and Contract Documents for work and investigations. * Assisting in the preparation of documentation for statutory approvals, such as planning permissions and wayleaves. * Collaborate with senior engineers and project managers during construction stage contract administration. * Gaining exposure to developing detailed project plans, including budgets, schedules, and resource allocation. * Assisting in ensuring projects are delivered within budget, on time, and in compliance with regulations and standards. * Learning to identify and mitigate project risks under guidance from senior team members. * Fostering effective communication and collaboration within the team, clients, and stakeholders. * Ensuring all work adheres to Health \& Safety legislation and quality management systems. * Undertake additional responsibilities as required, in line with the evolving needs of the role and the business. * Opportunities to visit sites across Ireland/UK. **Key Requirements** * Bachelor’s degree in Civil or Environmental Engineering or a related discipline (or currently completing). * Interest in and basic understanding of water engineering principles and practices. * Strong communication skills, both verbal and written, with the ability to work effectively as part of a collaborative team and build positive working relationships. * Methodical, detail\-oriented approach to problem\-solving, with curiosity, a willingness to ask questions, and an eagerness to learn through mentorship. * Self\-motivated and dependable, able to take initiative on assigned tasks while contributing positively within a supportive team environment. * Familiarity with design software such as AutoCAD, Civil 3D, GIS (ArcGIS \& QGIS) or similar tools is advantageous but not essential. * Familiarity with hydraulic modelling software such as Epanet, Synergi, MicroDrainage, HEC\-RAS or similar tools is advantageous but not essential. * Possession of a valid Irish driving license would be advantageous for site visits and project\-related travel. **About Us** Our mission is to deliver sustainable solutions that transform communities and bring about lasting positive change. Through a blend of expertise, innovation and global dedication, we envision a world where sustainable engineering protects growing communities. Nicholas O’Dwyer provides professional services in engineering design, project management, environment and planning, construction supervision and technical and contract advisory. We are one of the most respected Irish consultancies operating in local and international markets, providing professional services to public\- and private\-sector clients. Whether in Ireland, the UK or internationally, we understand and solve major challenges associated with delivering and adapting infrastructure for an increasing global population while maintaining a sustainable environment for future generations. Nicholas O’Dwyer is part of the RSK Group, a leading integrated environmental, engineering, and technical services business providing dynamic, multidisciplinary services to a wide range of sectors. RSK was founded in 1989 and has grown considerably since then. At RSK, we operate as a group of businesses that work together seamlessly, aiming for growth through diversification. Our strategy helps us build a strong and resilient business, capable of weathering external challenges, while also providing greater value to our clients and more opportunities for our employees. Since the beginning, our philosophy has remained the same: employ, develop and reward talented individuals. People are crucial to our company, and we recognise that our success hinges on our colleagues' growth. We're committed to preserving RSK's people\-first culture, promoting innovation, and fostering a supportive and caring environment for collaboration. At RSK, we want you to be part of our successes and help you reach your career aspirations. We take pride in offering employees fulfilling and challenging careers by promoting ongoing growth and empowering individuals to excel. We are committed to fostering an inclusive workplace that welcomes individuals from all backgrounds, experiences, and perspectives. We firmly believe that embracing and celebrating the diversity in our workforce makes us a stronger and more innovative company. If you're passionate about making a difference, join us as we work together to build a brighter, more equitable, and sustainable future for all. Do you have what it takes?

title: Graduate Mechanical Design Engineer – Dublin
description: * Permanent position available with a considerable portfolio of works for the next 5\+ years * To build your career by assisting in the delivery of ‘leading edge’ engineering projects. * To work with a vibrant, agile and multi\-functional team in delivering projects on time, safely, to budget and to the required quality standards. * To benefit from Kirby’s Career Development Programmes that will enhance your leadership capability. * To work with the best. * Excellent package on offer with room for negotiations **About Kirby Group Engineering** Kirby Group Engineering is an international multi\-disciplinary engineering services contractor and leading provider of high\-value engineering services to a number of ‘blue chip’ companies. Operating across Ireland, the UK, Europe and South Africa, Kirby has become the engineering service provider of choice in growth segments such as pharmaceuticals, data centres and power transmission and distribution. Our culture is innovative, collaborative and performance focused. The successful candidate will have the opportunity to grow and develop in a company committed to developing talent and rewarding performance. **Role Purpose** The role of a Graduate Mechanical Design Engineer is to execute engineering and design activities of the project. To conduct design reviews, project engineering, commissioning support ensuring consistent application of agreed procedures and utilization of standard forms. Graduate Mechanical Design Engineer’s Roles \& Responsibilities **General** * Assist and conduct the mechanical engineering and design activities on the project. * Support mechanical package owners. * Ensure the design management process is being followed on the project by either taking on the role directly. **EHS** * Read and understand EHS Plan. * Read, understand and promote the mechanical safe systems of work (MSSW) **Procurement** * Assist in the production of pre\-qualification packs (PQP) for design services and RFQ’s for equipment. Forward to project quantity surveyor for Subcontractor Package or Procurement Manager for Purchases. **RFQ Shall Include** * Scope of Works * Drawings and Specifications * Compliance, Deviation and Exclusion Matrices * Schedule – deliveries, installation and commissioning requirements PQQ shall be in line with MP17 – Selection and appointment of design consultants. * Mark\-up equipment and design services quotes to advise the project manager of the correct scope that aligns with the technical submittal and design brief. * Develop and update procurement \& design schedule/tracker with project planner. Continuously update the tracker until all technical submittals achieve status A, design deliverables accepted and materials/equipment have been delivered. **Quality** * Be engaged and utilize the documentation workflow for the project. Documentation generation, peer review, submission, re\-submission, distribution etc. * Review, comment and understand Quality Plan. * Assist in the development the project scope specific ITP considering project specification, client and local requirements. * Assist in the development of system tracker including equipment detail, interfacing details (electrical \& control), commissioning checklists and walkdown requirements * Review and understand any local authority requirements. * Develop lessons learned log of the project and continually update. * Indicate what the learning was * What the outcome or mitigations were * Indicate whether it was an internal finding or should be reported externally * Indicate if it was a cost, time or both impacts. * Assist in the O\&Ms production in line with the specification and agreed program. **Planning** * Understand and provide input and updates to the project schedule. * Input ROJ dates of CFCI equipment onto the equipment tracker. * Request and monitor the OFCI delivery dates. **Engineering \& Design** * Complete familiarisation and technical reviews on projects and record notes. Pose questions to the estimators and submit relevant RFI’s for clarifications on design/ scope. * Assist in the development of systems tracker along with equipment tag references, details and interfaces. * Assist in the development and tracking of design responsibility matrix/ contractor design portion (CDP). * Assist with the development and input into Design Deviation and Exclusions Log to ensure the team are aware of the agreed deviations and exclusions from design. * Validate the design using the request for information (RFI), design review process and performing the appropriate high level technical calculations. * Assist in the development of schematic’s when gaps exist in the design. * Review and understand The BIM execution plan, the Commissioning Plan and the Tracking Plan for the project. * Review the federated BIM model. * Review the coordinated drawings for construction to ensure it is in compliance with the P\&ID’s and schematics and that there is sufficient access to plant and equipment for commissioning, maintenance and replacement. * Development and track Technical Submittals until Status A. * Assist in the management of change from a technical perspective, updating change logs and schedules as required. * Tag Devices and instrumentation, spot each device on site in consultation with controls vendor and designer. * Attend biweekly technical meetings with members of the design team, subcontractors and internal project engineering team. * Assist in the verification of pipe and pump, duct and fan sizing and ensure design selections are suitable. * Assist in the production of the O\&M manuals in compliance with the specification. * Execute the project design and engineering process to ensure compliance. * Assist in technical review of tenders when required. * Attend and contribute to engineering and design kick\-off meeting at the outset of the project and record the minutes when required. * Attend and contribute to weekly engineering and design meetings and record the minutes when required. * Own action items identified during meetings and close them in a timely manner. **Communication** * Contribute to the development of weekly engineering and design reports and trackers. * Populate the project risk log and highlight any risks that arise to the project manager. * Manage and resolve risks and potential roadblocks title: Graduate Programme - Insurance - September 2026
description: By joining Sedgwick, you'll be part of something truly meaningful. It’s what our 33,000 colleagues do every day for people around the world who are facing the unexpected. We invite you to grow your career with us, experience our caring culture, and enjoy work\-life balance. Here, there’s no limit to what you can achieve. Newsweek Recognizes Sedgwick as America’s Greatest Workplaces National Top Companies Certified as a Great Place to Work® Fortune Best Workplaces in Financial Services \& Insurance Graduate Programme \- Insurance \- September 2026 **Graduate Loss Adjuster Programme (Ireland)** **Start your career in casualty \& liability claims with Sedgwick** Are you a recent graduate looking for a career where no two days are the same? Want to build real expertise while helping people through challenging situations? Our **Graduate Loss Adjuster Programme** is designed to give you everything you need to become a confident, skilled casualty or liability adjuster. **What You’ll Get** * Structured training * Learn the fundamentals of loss adjusting, including handling casualty and liability claims * Mentorship from day one * Be paired with an experienced adjuster who will guide and support your development * Professional qualification support * We’ll support you to achieve your APA insurance qualification (January 2027 sitting) * Real experience * Work on live claims and build practical skills from the start **What The Role Involves** As a Graduate Loss Adjuster, you will: * Support the management of casualty and liability claims from start to finish * Work with customers, insurers and third parties to investigate claims * Gather and assess information to understand what happened and what is covered * Help agree fair settlements * Keep accurate records and prepare reports * Build relationships with clients and stakeholders * Learn how to manage your own caseload over time You’ll split your time between **field visits** (homes or business premises) and **office\-based work** , developing both technical and people skills. **Why this role is different** * You’ll help people during difficult situations (e.g. injury claims or liability disputes) * You’ll develop investigation, negotiation and problem\-solving skills * You’ll gain a clear career path in insurance and claims * You’ll be part of a supportive, experienced team **Who We’re Looking For** We’re looking for graduates who are: * Curious and eager to learn * Strong communicators (clear, confident and empathetic) * Organised and detail\-focused * Good problem\-solvers * Interested in building a long\-term career in insurance / claims **Requirements** * Degree in Law, Business, Construction or Criminology * Full clean driving licence (essential for site visits) * Willingness to travel across Ireland when required **Why join Sedgwick** * Career growth – clear progression and development opportunities * Supportive environment – mentorship and structured learning * Meaningful work – make a real difference to people and businesses * Inclusive culture – a workplace that values diversity and fairness Sedgwick is an Equal Opportunity Employer. The statements contained in this document are intended to describe the general nature and level of work being performed by a colleague assigned to this description. They are not intended to constitute a comprehensive list of functions, duties, or local variances. Sedgwick retains the discretion to add or to change the duties of the position at any time.

title: Graduate Sales Programme
description: **Location: Sandyford, Dublin** **Full\-time role** **Salary: €30,000 \+ commission** **The Opportunity** Join an accredited immersive eighteen\-month graduate programme that will give you exposure to work across our various brands in the utilities sector, including PrepayPower and Yuno Energy — leaders in the utility sector in Ireland. The programme provides valuable sales experience, operational exposure and allows you to build professional and personal skills to forge a career in Sales or beyond. **Overview** The programme is designed to prepare you for successful careers in sales through rotations, coaching, and hands\-on experience. The role offers a unique blend of telesales and field sales experience, providing exposure to multiple sales channels and a strong foundation for a long\-term career in sales. After successfully completing the programme, there will be the possibility to progress to a commercial team. **Who Is It For?** * Recent graduates (0–2 years' experience) * Degrees in Business, Marketing, Economics, or any discipline with strong commercial acumen * Desired skills: * + Communication \& persuasion + Resilience \& adaptability + Curiosity \& learning agility + Results\-driven mindset ### ### **Benefits** * **Competitive salary and bonus structure** * **Private Pension Contribution Scheme** with Zurich Life * **Discounted Health Insurance \- Group Scheme** with Laya Healthcare * **Employee Assistance Program** provided by TELUS Health * **Free Online GP Services** via Webdoctor.ie * **Excellent career progression opportunities** in a growing company with strong market presence * **Fast\-paced, friendly team environment** * **Continuous training and development** * **Annual leave that increases with service** * **Exclusive PrepayPower Staff Tariff** * **Modern offices** with excellent facilities in Sandyford * **Active CSR, Wellness, and Social initiatives** plus great social events * **Refer\-a\-Friend Scheme** * **TaxSaver Travel Tickets \& Bike\-to\-Work Scheme** ### **About Yuno Energy** Since entering the Irish market in late 2023, we've been transforming the residential energy sector with a bold mission: to help consumers save energy and money through a data\-driven, app\-first experience. We are redefining how people understand and engage with their energy. Our innovative approach delivers radical transparency and rewards energy efficiency. With just a few taps, customers can gain a clear view of their energy habits—tracking every hour, unit, and cent of their usage. In 2025, together with PrepayPower, we proudly reached over 350,000 customer connections. **Why Work at Yuno Energy** At Yuno Energy, we believe work should be both challenging and rewarding. Ranked among Ireland's Great Places to Work*,* we offer ambitious individuals a dynamic, fast\-paced environment with exciting opportunities for growth. We are proud to be an Equal Opportunity Employer, committed to treating all employees, customers, and stakeholders with dignity and respect. We value diverse talent and are dedicated to building an inclusive workplace that drives innovation and delivers exceptional services. We encourage applications from people of all backgrounds and experiences. Yuno Energy is part of the Yuno Group, alongside PrepayPower and firmus Energy. With almost 600 employees and growing, we are shaping the future of electricity, gas, and broadband in Ireland. Join us and become part of a dynamic, ambitious team that's shaping the future of energy. **Privacy** By applying for this role, you agree to Yuno Energy processing your personal data for recruitment purposes. We handle all applications in line with our Privacy Notice You can view our Careers Privacy Policy \| Ireland's Newest Electricity Provider \- Yuno Energy for more information.

title: IT Airport Engineer - Dublin Airport
description: **IT Airport Engineer \- Dublin Airport** Location: Dublin Airport, Ireland Contract Duration: 6\-Month Fixed\-Term Contract Salary: €30,000 per annum plus shift allowance €500 Hours: Rotational shift pattern covering 24x7 airport operation **About ESP Global Services** ESP Global Services is a leading global provider of technology support and managed services, delivering critical IT and infrastructure solutions across multiple industries worldwide. We are seeking Airport Engineers to join our dedicated on\-site team at Dublin Airport, supporting passenger processing technologies that are essential to airport and airline operations. This is an exciting opportunity to work in a fast\-paced, operationally critical environment where technology availability directly impacts the passenger journey. **About the Role** As an Airport Engineer, you will provide first\-line technical support, maintenance and operational assistance for passenger processing systems across the airport estate. You will be responsible for ensuring that passenger check\-in, self\-service kiosk and automated bag\-drop technologies remain fully operational and available to support airport operations. Working within a dedicated engineering team, you will diagnose and resolve hardware issues, perform preventative maintenance, support installations and upgrades, manage spare parts inventories and maintain accurate asset records. **Key Responsibilities** * Provide first\-line technical support for passenger processing systems, including: + Common Use Passenger Processing Systems (CUPPS) + Common Use Self\-Service (CUSS) Kiosks + Self\-Service Bag Drop (SSBD) equipment * Diagnose and resolve hardware faults, equipment failures and peripheral issues. * Perform preventative maintenance, inspections and cleaning activities to maximise equipment availability. * Replace and repair hardware components and field\-replaceable units. * Respond to incidents, service requests and operational alerts through the designated IT Service Management (ITSM) platform. * Support equipment installations, moves, additions and changes (IMAC activities). * Maintain accurate records of all supported equipment, including asset tags, serial numbers, locations, hardware changes, warranty information and maintenance history within the Configuration Management Database (CMDB), asset management and service management systems. * Ensure all equipment moves, additions, changes, repairs and replacements are accurately recorded and traceable throughout the asset lifecycle. * Manage spare parts inventory, warranty returns and stock control processes. * Work closely with airport operations, service desk and technical support teams to maintain agreed service levels. * Support continuous improvement initiatives and operational readiness across the airport environment. **Skills \& Experience** **Essential** * Previous experience in a desktop support, field engineering, hardware support or technical maintenance role. * Strong hardware troubleshooting and break\-fix experience. * Experience supporting Windows\-based devices, printers, scanners, touchscreens or self\-service technologies. * Experience using ServiceNow and/or other IT Service Management (ITSM) platforms. * Experience working within ticket\-driven support environments and to service level agreements (SLAs). * Ability to work independently in a customer\-facing operational environment. * Strong communication, organisational and problem\-solving skills. * Full driving licence and ability to travel between airport locations where required. **Desirable Only** * Experience within an airport, aviation, transport or other operationally critical environment. * Experience supporting kiosks, passenger processing systems or self\-service technologies. * Experience maintaining IT asset inventories, equipment records and Configuration Management Databases (CMDBs). * Experience supporting asset lifecycle management and inventory control processes. * Previous airside operational experience. **Shift Pattern** This role forms part of a dedicated engineering team providing support across a 24x7 airport operation. Candidates must be flexible to work a rotating shift pattern, including days, nights, weekends and public holidays, as required to maintain service coverage and operational resilience. **Security \& Airside Requirements** Due to airport security and airside access requirements, all successful candidates must be capable of obtaining and maintaining an airport security pass. Applicants must be able to provide a fully verifiable five\-year employment, education and address history. Any significant unexplained gaps, or periods that cannot be satisfactorily evidenced through references or supporting documentation, may affect eligibility for airport security clearance and airside access approval. **What We Offer** * Annual Salary of €30,000 plus shift allowance (pro\-rated) * Comprehensive product and technical training. * Opportunity to work within one of Ireland's busiest and most dynamic operational environments. * Experience supporting business\-critical passenger processing technologies. * Opportunity to work alongside a dedicated team of airport technology professionals. * Exposure to airport operations, infrastructure and passenger\-facing technologies. * Potential contract extension opportunities based on business requirements and performance. If you are a customer\-focused engineer who enjoys solving technical problems in fast\-paced operational environments and wants to play a key role in keeping passengers moving, we would like to hear from you. ESP Global Services is an equal opportunities employer and welcomes applications from all suitably qualified candidates.

title: IT Operations Technician
description: Red Chair Recruitment is currently seeking an IT Operations Technician to support and maintain critical IT infrastructure within a complex, mission\-critical environment. This senior\-level position involves providing 2nd and 3rd line support, managing infrastructure services, and delivering IT projects across enterprise environments. **Key Responsibilities** * Provide 2nd and 3rd line support across core infrastructure and enterprise systems. * Manage and maintain Windows Server environments and Active Directory services. * Lead ICT infrastructure projects and system deployments. * Build, configure, and support server environments and application platforms. * Support networking infrastructure, including firewalls and wireless technologies. * Manage SAN storage and Fibre Channel environments. * Support cybersecurity tools, monitoring systems, and security operations. * Liaise with external vendors and third\-party service providers. **Key Technologies** * Windows Server \& Active Directory * VMware vSphere / vCenter * Cisco networking \& ASA firewalls * SAN Storage \& Fibre Channel * Veeam Backup * LAN, WAN, and VLAN networking **Requirements** * Degree in IT, Computer Science, or a related discipline. * Proven experience supporting enterprise IT infrastructure environments. * Strong knowledge of Windows Server, networking, and virtualisation technologies. * Experience delivering infrastructure projects and major deployments. * Relevant certifications such as CCNA, CCNP, or MCP are desirable. * Excellent problem\-solving, analytical, and communication skills.

title: IT Support Technician
description: **Job Summary** Company: Live Nation Ireland Holdings Limited Location: 3Arena and Bord Gáis Energy Theatre Reports to: Manager, Information Technology Working Hours: Full time Job Title: Permanent **Role Description** To provide IT technical support to employees across the different departments and sites of the organisation. Reporting to the Manager, Information Technology, they will also be involved in the roll\-out and implementation of new services. **Who You Are** **Competencies / Skills / Knowledge / Experience** * Third level qualification in Information Technology NFQ Level 6 or equivalent * At least one of the following certifications: A\+, Network \+, MTA Networking Fundamentals, MTA Security Fundamentals * Solid post qualification experience is required for this role * Strong knowledge of MS networking technologies – Active Directory, Group Policy, DNS, DHCP * Strong knowledge of Exchange/Outlook * Some experience administrating switches, firewalls and routers * Knowledge of VPN and WAN technologies advantageous * PBX and VOIP technologies an advantage * Excellent written and verbal communication skills **Behaviours** The following attributes determine how the role will be carried out and are required to be a success * Strong use of initiative * Demonstrate the ability to plan and organise * High degree of flexibility * Good problem solver and decision maker **What The Role Includes** * Provide first and second line support to end\-users on all ICT related tasks both over the phone and at desk side * Assist with maintaining IT Support systems are kept up to date * Desktop environment including hardware (PCs, printers, mobile devices, telecoms), applications and operating systems (Windows 7, 10\) * Troubleshoot and resolve complex hardware and software faults * Setup of new users on IT systems * Installation of IT hardware and software * Play an active role in new project as they arise and be involved in the change management process * Take ownership of IT projects when requested to successful completion * Train staff in the use of IT systems * Ensure confidentiality in all matters of information obtained during the course of employment * Present and act in a professional manner at all times * Be aware of current developments and keep abreast of new developments in ICT * Assume responsibility for his/her own professional development and safe work practice * Ensure a safe environment for yourself, colleagues and visitors * Perform such other duties appropriate to the post as may be assigned from time to time by the management or a nominee **Equal Opportunities** We are passionate and committed to our people and go beyond the rhetoric of diversity and inclusion. You will be working in an inclusive environment and be encouraged to bring your whole self to work. We will do all that we can to help you successfully balance your work and homelife. As a growing business we will encourage you to develop your professional and personal aspirations, enjoy new experiences, and learn from the talented people you will be working with. It’s talent that matters to us and we encourage applications from people irrespective of their gender identity, race, sexual orientation, religion, age, disability status or caring responsibilities. **The Company** Live Nation Entertainment is the world’s leading live entertainment company, comprised of global market leaders: Ticketmaster, Live Nation Concerts, and Live Nation Media \& Sponsorship. Ticketmaster is the global leader in event ticketing with over 500 million tickets sold annually and more than 12,000 clients worldwide. Live Nation Concerts is the largest provider of live entertainment in the world promoting more than 40,000 shows and 100\+ festivals annually for nearly 4,000 artists in over 40 countries. These businesses allow Live Nation Media \& Sponsorship to create strategic music marketing programs that connect over 1,000 brands with the 98 million fans that attend Live Nation Entertainment events each year. For additional information, visit www.livenationentertainment.com **APPLICATION DEADLINE: Monday 6th July 2026\.** We reserve the right to close applications at any time so encourage early application where possible. Live Nation Entertainment will never request payment or equipment purchases as part of the hiring process. Recruiters will only contact candidates from official Live Nation or affiliated brand email domains.

title: IT Systems Engineer
description: **Contract Opportunity \| IT Systems Engineer \| Dublin (Hybrid)** I’m working with a high growth tech business looking to hire an experienced IT Systems Engineer for an initial 6 month contract. This is a hands on role for someone who enjoys owning IT operations in a fast moving environment. You’ll take the lead across infrastructure, identity, endpoints, SaaS applications, and day to day IT support in a hybrid Google Workspace and Microsoft 365 environment. **What you’ll be doing:** • Managing identity and access across Google Workspace, Microsoft 365 and Entra ID • Supporting a broad SaaS estate with SSO and authentication • Managing Mac and Windows devices via Addigy and Intune • Owning onboarding and offboarding processes • Handling IT support across access, hardware, software and security issues • Supporting VPN, password management and security tooling • Driving automation and process improvements **What they’re looking for:** • Strong Google Workspace admin experience • Strong Microsoft 365 / Entra ID knowledge • Experience with SSO, SAML and OIDC • Solid MDM experience across Mac and Windows • Strong troubleshooting and support skills • Scripting experience in Python or PowerShell is a plus Bonus points for experience with Pritunl, Keeper, JumpCloud, or compliance frameworks like ISO 27001 / SOC2\. This would suit someone who likes solving problems, improving processes, and keeping systems running smoothly. 📍 Dublin hybrid 📅 6 month initial contract

title: In-Game Specialist (DNA)
description: **Who We Are** At 2K, we create some of the most iconic and culture\-shaping video games in entertainment, including ***NBA® 2K***, one of the top\-selling franchises in the world, and legendary titles like ***BioShock®****,* ***Borderlands®****,* ***Mafia****,* ***Sid Meier's Civilization*****®**, and***XCOM®***, as well as fan favorites ***WWE® 2K****,* ***TopSpin®***, and ***PGA TOUR® 2K****.* We build unforgettable experiences by pushing the boundaries of creativity, authenticity and innovation across every genre. Our portfolio is brought to life by some of the most influential game development studios in the world. **Visual Concepts, Firaxis Games, Hangar 13, Cat Daddy Games, 31st Union, Cloud Chamber, Gearbox, HB Studios,** and **2K SportsLab** create world\-class experiences across platforms. But what truly powers 2K is our people. We believe the best ideas come from teams that feel empowered, supported, and inspired. As an equal opportunity employer, we are committed to fostering a diverse, inclusive workplace where people are encouraged to **come as they are** and do their best work. **What We Need** The In\-Game Specialist is the operational and strategic lead for the in\-game messaging lifecycle within DNA titles. The Specialist operationalizes the in\-game weekly ad schedule and storefront offers. This role is responsible for the end\-to\-end orchestration of targeted marketing and lifecycle campaigns across messaging and commerce, including but not limited to upsell and player engagement journeys, partnership activations, and locker code delivery. Acting as a central hub between Marketing, Product, Insights, Joint Tech (T2/2KT) and Game Studios. In this remit, the specialist also plays an important role in the coordination of pre\- and post\-launch in\-game QA testing to ensure successful delivery of in\-game messaging and storefronts. A critical component of this P4 role involves scaling in\-game DNA tech capabilities in partnership with 2KT to unlock greater commercial levers as well as driving the continuous refinement of team processes to ensure successful delivery at scale. This individual provides functional mentorship to rotating graduates while driving operational excellence across the Owned Media team. **What You'll Do** The In\-Game Specialist is the operational and strategic lead for the in\-game messaging lifecycle within DNA titles. The Specialist operationalizes the in\-game weekly ad schedule and storefront offers. This role is responsible for the end\-to\-end orchestration of targeted marketing and lifecycle campaigns across messaging and commerce, including but not limited to upsell and player engagement journeys, partnership activations, and locker code delivery. Acting as a central hub between Marketing, Product, Insights, Joint Tech (T2/2KT) and Game Studios. In addition, the specialist plays an important role in the coordination of pre\- and post\-launch in\-game QA testing and the scaling of in\-game DNA tech capabilities. This individual provides functional mentorship to rotating graduates while driving operational excellence across the Owned Media team. **What Will Make You A Great Fit** Ad/Storefront Operations \& Strategy * Strategy \& Scheduling: Execute the weekly in\-game messaging calendar and set up in\-game storefront offers, as well as supporting a wide range of targeted upsell and engagement activations. * End\-to\-End Operationalization: Manage the technical setup, scheduling, and deployment of all in\-game messaging and storefronts, ensuring 100% accuracy in a high\-stakes, live\-service environment. * Performance Optimization: Partner with the Insights team to understand impact of in\-game interventions and refine in\-game strategy. Process Refinement \& Scalability * Workflow Optimization: Evaluate and refine existing team processes to eliminate bottlenecks and ensure the successful delivery of activations as the portfolio and complexity scale. * Standardization: Develop and maintain standardized templates, documentation, and playbooks for in\-game ad and storefront operations. * Scalability Initiatives: Identify and implement tools or automated solutions that increase the team's capacity to manage high\-volume activations. DNA Tech Scalability * Strategy \& Workflow Optimization: Identifying strategic opportunities to unlock commercial \& engagement levers and streamline internal workstreams through DNA tech. * Tech Briefing: Submitting detailed tech briefs for new feature development \& improvements to existing features and submitting bugs on an ongoing basis to develop DNA tech capabilities. * Scalability \& Standardization: Ensure long term scalability of all tech requests and standardization across all DNA integrated titles. Product/Marketing \& Developer Collaboration * Product/Marketing Integration: Collaborate closely with the BU Product \& Marketing teams to synchronize in\-game activations with external campaigns, partnerships, and cross\-sell initiatives. * Studio Developer Liaison: Act as the primary operational point of contact for studios such as HB Studios, Hangar 13, VC (WWE), and Firaxis. * Technical Troubleshooting: Work directly with developer studios on bug identification and the continuous optimization of in\-game tech to improve the player experience. Functional Leadership * Mentorship: Oversee the day\-to\-day work of rotating graduates, providing task\-level guidance and ensuring operational excellence. * Functional Expert: Serve as the subject matter expert for in\-game operations, advising internal stakeholders on feasibility and best practices. Core Competencies * Behavioral: Strong sense of urgency and ownership; ability to maintain high precision under tight weekly deadlines. * Functional / Technical: Expert knowledge of in\-game messaging \& commerce technology (Technodrome), targeted campaign frameworks, and tech briefing. * Operational Excellence \& Scalability: Proven ability to build scalable workflows and improve organizational efficiency. * Strategic Influence: Ability to align cross\-functional stakeholders (Marketing, Product, Dev, Insights) toward a unified in\-game strategy. * Collaboration: Proven ability to build strong working relationships with marketing, product, and technical teams alike. Required Qualifications, Knowledge, and Job\-Related Skills * Extensive experience in digital operations, live\-service gaming, or in\-game messaging management. * Proven experience in process improvement or operational scaling within a marketing or technical environment. * Track record of managing a high\-volume, weekly operational cadence. * Experience in working with technical teams to deliver scalable UI \& functionality product improvements * Understanding of video game launch \& release management processes and experience working with Functional QA teams to validate player\-facing product features * Strong analytical skills with the ability to interpret data insights and pivot strategy accordingly * Excellent project management skills, specifically in coordinating between Marketing and Technical/Development teams. * Self\-motivated with the ability to operate independently at a P4 seniority level. Preferred Qualifications * Passion for our player's experience in\-game and an understanding of video games, as well as key marketing principles. * Experience working within a global publishing organization. As an equal opportunity employer, we are committed to ensuring that qualified individuals with disabilities are provided reasonable accommodation to participate in the job application or interview process, to perform their essential job functions, and to receive other benefits and privileges of employment. Please contact us if you need reasonable accommodation. Please note that 2K Games and its studios never uses instant messaging apps or personal email accounts to contact prospective employees or conduct interviews and when emailing, only use 2K.com accounts. \#LI\-Hybrid

title: Incident Management Engineer (Spanish/English bilingual), Incident Detection and Response
description: **DESCRIPTION** --------------- Amazon has built a reputation for excellence with a mission to be the earth’s most customer\-centric company, a company that customers from all over the globe will recognize, value, and trust for both our products and our service. Amazon Web Services (AWS) is carrying on that tradition while leading the world in cloud technologies. The AWS Incident Detection and Response team is part of the ASPIRE organisation within AWS Support, and is dedicated to offering eligible AWS Enterprise Support customers proactive engagement and incident management to reduce the potential for failure and to accelerate recovery of critical workloads from disruption. We achieve these objectives by working closely with customers to develop runbooks and response plans customized to the context of each workload onboarded to the service. Onboarded workloads are monitored 24x7 by a team of Incident Management Engineers (IMEs) to detect and engage customers on a call bridge within 5 minutes of a critical alarm. ABOUT YOU Incident Management Engineers have a broad skill set with demonstrated career progression and a proven track record of delivering results. The successful candidate will possess strong analytical acumen, solid technology experience, superb business judgment, strategic account ownership and a propensity to dive deep to solve complex problems. You will also have a passion for creating/providing a world class experience for our customers. The candidate must understand the competitive and industry landscape and must have the leadership presence and communication skills to effectively work with customers at all levels of their organization. You must be a self\-starter and able to execute at both a tactical and strategic level – with a strong attention to detail. This is a global role that requires excellent written and verbal communication skills and a passion and desire for leading the resolution of critical incidents. Your decisions are not only fundamental to helping protect our most critical customers but will help maintain the health of AWS customers worldwide. Finally, you are passionate about technology with a desire to learn more and do more with AWS. ABOUT THE ROLE AWS Support is looking for a leader with a strong background in Incident Management and customer ownership to be there during the moments that matter for our most critical customers. We are looking for an Incident Management Engineer to join our team to provide incident response and account ownership. In this position, you will play a pivotal role in providing communication, emergency response, technical resolver engagement and incident management for our customers. Please note as a follow\-the\-sun organisation, IMEs work the core hours of 7am\-3pm UTC. Successful applicants will be required to work some weekends (Sunday to Thursday, or Tuesday to Saturday), and public holidays. Key job responsibilities * Every day will bring new and exciting challenges that include elements of: * Drive the resolution of large scale customer impacting incidents as part of a team rotation * Drive critical, complex customer escalations in situations that are sometimes technically challenging in collaboration with Engineering Teams. * Provide critical incident response/management (including leading calls with internal/external participants) for customer’s critical workloads * Contribute to Problem Records for customers * Conduct continuous real\-time proactive monitoring of customer metrics * Prioritize, manage, and own emerging and developing customer issues from start to finish * Monitor and manage communications during high impact events via relevant channels * Collaborate with key stakeholders across AWS to improve the customer experience and develop mechanisms that support operational excellence * Lead projects and teams to drive operational improvements * Create and review documentation; design/influence new standard operating procedures * Identify and troubleshoot recurring platform issues and own projects to drive improvements * Mentor peers in your areas of technical and operational strength * Perform other duties as required by the organization About the team Diverse Experiences AWS values diverse experiences. Even if you do not meet all of the preferred qualifications and skills listed in the job description, we encourage candidates to apply. If your career is just starting, hasn’t followed a traditional path, or includes alternative experiences, don’t let it stop you from applying. Why AWS? Amazon Web Services (AWS) is the world’s most comprehensive and broadly adopted cloud platform. We pioneered cloud computing and never stopped innovating — that’s why customers from the most successful startups to Global 500 companies trust our robust suite of products and services to power their businesses. Inclusive Team Culture Here at AWS, it’s in our nature to learn and be curious. Our employee\-led affinity groups foster a culture of inclusion that empower us to be proud of our differences. Ongoing events and learning experiences, including our Conversations on Race and Ethnicity (CORE) and AmazeCon (diversity) conferences, inspire us to never stop embracing our uniqueness. Mentorship \& Career Growth We’re continuously raising our performance bar as we strive to become Earth’s Best Employer. That’s why you’ll find endless knowledge\-sharing, mentorship and other career\-advancing resources here to help you develop into a better\-rounded professional. Work/Life Balance We value work\-life harmony. Achieving success at work should never come at the expense of sacrifices at home, which is why we strive for flexibility as part of our working culture. When we feel supported in the workplace and at home, there’s nothing we can’t achieve in the cloud. **BASIC QUALIFICATIONS** ------------------------ * Speak, write, and read fluently in Spanish * Bachelor's degree in computer science or equivalent, or experience in technical support * 4\+ years of technical support experience * 3\+ years demonstrable Major Incident / Problem Manager Experience for organizations that run mission critical applications * 4\+ years of experience in Support Engineering, Network Engineering, Solutions Architecture, or similar IT role **PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS** ---------------------------- * Industry specific accredited certification(s) such as the AWS Associate level certifications * Knowledge of ITIL/Lean Processes * Ability to work in ambiguous environments * Demonstrated critical thinking and logical problem solving skills * Experience driving collaborative projects from conception to delivery * Familiarity operating or designing distributed architectures with the ability to correlate system behaviors based on known inter\-dependencies * Ability to review complex technical details regarding ongoing issues/events and convey the key details to senior stakeholders to facilitate real\-time decision making Amazon is an equal opportunities employer. We believe passionately that employing a diverse workforce is central to our success. We make recruiting decisions based on your experience and skills. We value your passion to discover, invent, simplify and build. Protecting your privacy and the security of your data is a longstanding top priority for Amazon. Please consult our Privacy Notice (https://www.amazon.jobs/en/privacy\_page) to know more about how we collect, use and transfer the personal data of our candidates. Amazon is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of protected veteran status, disability, or other legally protected status. Our inclusive culture empowers Amazonians to deliver the best results for our customers. If you have a disability and need a workplace accommodation or adjustment during the application and hiring process, including support for the interview or onboarding process, please visit https://amazon.jobs/content/en/how\-we\-hire/accommodations for more information. If the country/region you’re applying in isn’t listed, please contact your Recruiting Partner.

title: Incident Response Analyst II
description: **Job Overview** The Incident Response Analyst is a key member of the Incident Response Center (IRC), responsible for 24/7 monitoring, first\-level analysis, triage, and escalation of alerts related to data center infrastructure and services. This role ensures continuous health, availability, and security of data center operations by monitoring critical systems, detecting events, assessing impact, and coordinating with cross\-functional teams for timely resolution. The Analyst provides real\-time situational awareness across data center facilities, network and server systems, environmental controls, cloud infrastructure, and external risk factors. **Key Responsibilities** * Monitor alarms and alerts across data center infrastructure, including network and server devices, hardware health indicators (disk usage, temperature, etc.), WAN circuits, local connectivity, and facility environmental systems such as temperature, humidity, power, racks, and PDUs. * Monitor Oracle Cloud Infrastructure (OCI), cloud dashboards, and relevant monitoring tools for system health and service availability. * Assess external risks such as natural events (floods, earthquakes, fires, severe weather) that may impact data center operations. * Monitor and review CCTV activity for security events within data center facilities. * Validate, investigate, and analyze alerts to determine impact and urgency. * Create, update, and manage incident tickets using Jira or similar ITSM platforms. * Escalate incidents promptly to cross\-functional teams including Network, Compute, Storage, Facilities, Security, and Cloud teams. * Communicate incident updates and notifications using tools such as Everbridge and email. * Prepare Post\-Incident Reviews (PIRs), document timelines, and maintain accurate incident records and handover notes. * Review and track vendor emails regarding planned maintenance, emergency changes, and service advisories. * Maintain documentation including SOPs, process updates, and knowledge base articles. **Basic Qualifications** * Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, Engineering, or related field, or equivalent practical experience. * 2–5 years of experience in a data center operations center, NOC/SOC, monitoring environment, or incident management role. * Basic understanding of network fundamentals (TCP/IP, routing, WAN/LAN concepts) and server or hardware components (CPU, memory, storage). * Ability to interpret system alerts, analyze issues, and perform initial triage. * Excellent verbal and written communication skills, with the ability to work independently, meet goals, and maintain attention to detail. * Demonstrated ability to interact effectively at all levels within the organization, including with clients, while being a collaborative team player. * Ability to work in 24/7 rotating shifts as required. **Preferred Qualifications** * Experience in a Data Center, NOC, SOC, or similar operations command center environment. * Familiarity with tools such as Jira, Everbridge, DCIM systems, monitoring platforms (SolarWinds, Nagios, Grafana, etc.), and CCTV monitoring tools. * Experience writing incident reports or post\-incident reviews. * Exposure to cloud platforms such as Oracle Cloud Infrastructure, AWS, Azure, or GCP. * Knowledge of ITIL processes, especially Incident, Problem, and Change Management. * Strong situational awareness and ability to manage multiple priorities during critical events. * Effective collaboration skills when working with cross\-functional support teams.

title: InfoSec Analyst I
description: MongoDB’s Security Assurance team is on a mission to make MongoDB one of the world’s most trusted technology vendors. We are doing this in two ways. First, we provide customers with the clear, concise and accurate information about the security of our products that customers require to use our products. Second, we serve as trusted security SMEs to MongoDB’s field teams. The InfoSec Analyst I is an independent contributor role. This role is vital to the Security Assurance team’s mission and is responsible for coordinating completion of customer due diligence reviews as well as helping MongoDB’s field personnel answer complex customer security questions. We are looking to speak to candidates who are based in Dublin for our hybrid working model. Responsibilities: * Actively manage the Assurance team’s queue to ensure requests are handled promptly * Complete customer security reviews. Help other team members complete reviews as necessary * Help customer facing personnel respond to customer security questions * Monitor relevant channels and answer in scope questions when possible, or escalate to more senior team members * Proactively maintain the team’s knowledgebase * Proactively recommend improvements in team processes and tooling to team leadership * Share relevant and actionable customer feedback with team leadership * Help coordinate development of customer facing response to high\-profile supply chain events including critical severity CVEs that may affect MongoDB or its vendors * Lead complex projects to help build, scale and train the team * Serve as an internal subject matter expert on MongoDB product security topics, assurance trends, customer concerns, and emerging regulations * Design and deliver internal enablement training on security posture and compliance topics for teams across the organization Requirements: * 2 to 3 years of experience in Information Security * BS in Computer Science, Information Systems, or equivalent experience * Direct exposure to at least one major framework or regulation such as ISO 27001, SOC2, CSA or DORA is preferred * Basic understanding of public cloud technologies including IAM, encryption, network security and tenant isolation * Strong communication skills, with the ability to present security concepts to technical and non\-technical stakeholders up to and including at the executive levels * Ability to coordinate and manage multiple urgent requests simultaneously while effectively collaborating with SMEs across various time zones * A self\-starter with a strong desire to grow into a trusted authority on the security of MongoDB and its products Success in this Role Means: * Within 3 months \- Have a solid understanding of MongoDB’s product portfolio, core security posture, and the Security Assurance team’s procedures and tooling; reliably triage incoming requests; independently complete customer security reviews with guidance from more senior team members; monitor key internal channels and answer straightforward in‑scope questions * Within 6 months \- Independently own end‑to‑end completion of most standard customer security reviews; serve as a trusted partner to customer‑facing teams by helping them respond to complex security questions; make measurable contributions to the team knowledgebase and playbooks; proactively identify and help implement improvements to team processes and tooling * Within 12 months \- Be recognized as a trusted subject matter expert on MongoDB’s product portfolio, security posture and common customer concerns; complete complex projects that materially improve review quality, throughput, or customer experience; consistently surface relevant, actionable customer feedback that can influence Security roadmaps **About MongoDB** MongoDB is built for change, empowering our customers and our people to innovate at the speed of the market. We have redefined the data platform for the AI era, enabling builders to create, transform, and disrupt industries with software. MongoDB’s unified data platform, the most widely available, globally distributed data platform on the market, helps organizations modernize legacy workloads, embrace innovation, and unleash AI. Our cloud\-native platform, MongoDB Atlas, is the only globally distributed, multi\-cloud data platform and is available across AWS, Google Cloud, and Microsoft Azure. With offices worldwide and over 67,000 customers, including 75% of the Fortune 100 and AI\-native startups, relying on MongoDB for their most important applications, we’re powering the next era of software. Our compass at MongoDB is our Leadership Commitment, guiding how and why we make decisions, show up for each other, and win. It’s what makes us MongoDB. To drive the personal growth and business impact of our employees, we’re committed to developing a supportive and enriching culture for everyone. From employee affinity groups, to fertility assistance and a generous parental leave policy, we value our employees’ wellbeing and want to support them along every step of their professional and personal journeys. Learn more about what it’s like to work at MongoDB, and help us make an impact on the world! MongoDB is committed to providing any necessary accommodations for individuals with disabilities within our application and interview process. To request an accommodation due to a disability, please inform your recruiter. MongoDB is an equal opportunities employer. *Req ID: 2273456141*

title: Information Communication Technology Support Engineer
description: **ICT Support Engineer** PFH Technology are currently looking for an ICT Support Engineer to join our growing team. **Role Overview:** We are seeking an experienced ICT Support Engineer to support the implementation and operation of a major national Laboratory Information Management System (LIMS) programme across healthcare sites in Ireland. This is a field\-based role requiring travel to healthcare facilities within an assigned geographical region. The successful candidate will provide onsite technical support, device configuration, troubleshooting, and stakeholder engagement to ensure successful deployment and ongoing operation of a critical healthcare technology platform. **Key Responsibilities:** * Provide onsite technical support across one or multiple healthcare locations within an assigned region. * Work closely with programme teams and local implementation leads to deliver deployment activities in line with implementation schedules. * Configure and support desktop, laptop, mobile, and peripheral devices for use with the laboratory information system. * Troubleshoot hardware, software, and basic network issues, managing incidents through to resolution. * Escalate complex technical or application issues to appropriate technical teams and managers. * Perform system administration tasks within the laboratory information system environment. * Liaise with third\-party vendors for hardware and software installations and support. * Assess site readiness and conduct hardware assessments for system deployment. * Ensure compatibility between hardware, operating systems, applications, and laboratory system requirements. * Support mobile device configuration and management. * Contribute to technical documentation and knowledge base resources. * Build strong working relationships with healthcare staff, ICT teams, and programme stakeholders. * Coordinate with service providers and local ICT departments to ensure successful implementations. * Deliver excellent customer service and user support throughout deployment and operational phases. **Skills \& Experience:** * Experience providing onsite ICT support in a healthcare or enterprise environment. * Strong troubleshooting skills across hardware, software, and operating systems. * Knowledge of desktop, laptop, peripheral, and mobile device configuration. * Experience supporting Windows environments and common business applications. * Basic understanding of networking concepts and troubleshooting. * Ability to manage multiple priorities and work independently. * Excellent communication and stakeholder engagement skills. * **Full driving licence** and willingness to travel regularly between sites. PFH Technology, owned by Ricoh – a Japanese based global ICT company, is a premier provider of end\-to\-end ICT solutions and a managed services portfolio scaling from SMEs to large Enterprise organisations. We have unrivalled vendor relationships. We can procure, design, deploy and support all your ICT needs. Our ISO certified Custodian™ Cloud Services and Custodian™ Managed Services provide the technology and expertise to mitigate risk and reduce your costs immediately. We have a nationwide network of over 750 dedicated professionals, ready to meet your ICT needs, with offices in Dublin, Cork and Galway. We are an equal opportunity employer and value diversity at PFH. We do not discriminate on the basis of age, disability, civil or family status, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, race, religion, or any other legally protected status. Please contact us if you require accommodation at any stage.

description: 1. **Control Your Earnings: Uncapped commission – the more you deliver, the more you take home.** 2. **Progress When You Perform: No waiting years.
