title: Junior IT Operations Engineer
description: **What’s the Opportunity** At Distilled, we're looking for a **Junior IT Operations / L1 Helpdesk Engineer** to join our team and help deliver a fantastic technology experience for our employees. This is a hands\-on role focused on supporting our people, maintaining our office technology, and ensuring employees have the tools and support they need to do their best work. You'll work closely with the IT Operations team, providing day\-to\-day support across hardware, software, meeting rooms, onboarding, and office technology while developing your skills across modern cloud\-based platforms such as Google Workspace, Okta, Jamf, Microsoft 365 and Freshservice. This role is ideal for someone who enjoys solving problems, helping people, learning new technologies and taking ownership of their work. **Please note:** This role requires **4 days per week onsite** at our centrally located Dublin office, with flexibility to work remotely on the remaining day. **What will I be doing** As a Junior IT Operations Engineer, you'll play a key role in supporting employees and maintaining the smooth operation of our technology environment. You'll work across a wide variety of technologies while building your skills and experience in a supportive team environment. * **Employee Support \& Service Delivery:** Providing remote and onsite IT support for employees across the business, troubleshooting hardware, software, application and device issues and responding to both walk\-up requests and helpdesk tickets. Assisting employees with Google Workspace, Okta, Microsoft 365 and other business applications, maintaining high levels of customer service and communication throughout, and escalating issues appropriately when required. * **Onboarding, Offboarding \& User Lifecycle:** Preparing laptops and equipment for new starters, including purchasing and shipping as needed. Assisting with employee onboarding and offboarding activities, including creating and updating user accounts, groups and permissions. Ensuring devices, applications and access are ready for Day 1, while maintaining accurate records of employee equipment and access. * **Office Technology \& Meeting Rooms:** Maintaining meeting room technology, AV equipment and shared workspaces, supporting company meetings, presentations and events. Troubleshooting issues with displays, cameras, microphones and video conferencing systems, ensure hotdesks and shared technology areas remain operational and well maintained, and be a visible and approachable IT presence in the office. * **Asset Management \& Inventory:** Maintaining accurate IT asset inventory records and assisting with equipment ordering and stock management, tracking hardware throughout its lifecycle. Organise and maintain the IT stock room, ensuring spare equipment and accessories are available when needed. * **Continuous Learning \& Improvement:** Continuously developing skills across the technology stack and contributing to documentation and knowledge base articles. Identify recurring issues and suggest improvements, and support automation and process improvement initiatives where appropriate. **What skills and experience do I need** * Minimum 1 years’ experience in an IT Support, Service Desk, Desktop Support or IT Operations role * Strong troubleshooting skills across Windows, MacOS or Mobile devices * Experience supporting Microsoft 365, Google Workspace or similar collaboration platforms * Experience with user account management and onboarding/offboarding activities * Experience with supporting video conference room setup * Strong communication and customer service skills * Ability to prioritise workload and manage multiple tasks * Positive attitude and willingness to learn * Strong organisational skills and attention to detail * Comfortable working onsite and interacting directly with employees **Advantage if you have** * Exposure to Intune, Jamf, Okta, Entra ID or other identity/device management platforms * Experience with Google Meet or Zoom Rooms * Experience maintaining asset inventory or equipment lifecycle processes * Basic networking knowledge (Wi\-Fi, VPNs, DNS, DHCP) * Experience with Freshservice, or similar ticketing systems * ITIL Foundation, Jamf 100, Google Workspace, Microsoft or CompTIA certifications **What’s it like to work here** You will have the opportunity to work with one of Ireland's fastest growing Internet companies. You will get the chance to create a significant contribution to our strategic direction. Projects are exciting and employees are given wide scope for creativity and innovation **Benefits \- what you can expect** * Our goal is to maintain a culture where people look forward to work – whether in our centrally located office in Dublin or working from home. * We do that by making Distilled a place you'll be listened to and trusted to do your job. Where you can have fun, learn and feel appreciated. And where you get focused career development, growth and progression opportunities. * We want you to feel confident that this is the right place for you, your career, your wellbeing and your future. * Above all, your health and wellbeing are at the forefront of how we build the business. That means looking after you while you're here and later in life. So you'll receive life insurance, fully\-paid healthcare and a 5% contribution towards your pension. **More about Distilled** We exist to make buying and selling in Ireland simple. We put our people first, creating an inclusive environment where we can develop products that put our users first. We value people’s work and time, creating a community where they have an opportunity to make an impact and grow. Our challenge is to enhance our market\-leading products using insights and empathy to present our users with the perfect match. What we build is technology, but what we do is create connections for life’s most meaningful moments. We are Distilled and we **bring people together**. **Our Values** We have five core values: We have a **growth mindset.** We like to **simplify things** and **make things happen**. We **collaborate** across teams, business units and brands. We're **passionate** about what we do. These values give us a framework for leadership and daily decision making. They embody how we behave and present ourselves — as individuals and as a business — both internally and externally for our colleagues, our users and our customers. *We are a diverse bunch of people here in Distilled, and we want to continue to attract and retain a diverse range of people into our Company. We do not discriminate based on gender, marital status, family status, sexual orientation, religion, age, disability, race and membership of the Traveller community.*

title: Junior Project Management Coordinator
description: We are adding a Junior Project Management Coordinator to our team to help us manage a series of technical projects for a partner in the international financial services sector. This role is about the practical side of delivery: making sure requirements are clear, keeping project tickets updated, and ensuring that our developers have exactly what they need to get their work done. This is a fully remote position, meaning you will work from your own space and coordinate with us through digital tools. **About Pixel Systems** Pixel Systems is a small software agency that builds the tools teams actually use. We do not build flashy social media apps or marketing landing pages. We focus on the practical stuff like admin panels, CRM integrations, and the database migrations that most teams try to avoid. Our clients come to us when they need to replace a sprawling spreadsheet with a real database or get different software tools to communicate with each other. We believe that working code is better than a perfect plan that never ships. Our approach is straightforward: we provide fixed quotes before we start, we do weekly demos, and we hand over everything when we are finished. This includes the code, the documentation, and even walkthrough videos for the client. We use stable, well\-tested technologies like Node, React, and Postgres because we want the software we build to be reliable. We are a small, distributed team that prefers solving real operational headaches over chasing the latest industry trends. **The Role** As a Junior Project Management Coordinator, your focus will be on the daily flow of information. You will be the person who translates client requests into clear tasks for our development team. Because our partner in the international financial services sector has precise data requirements, you will spend a lot of time organizing specifications and checking that nothing has been missed. Your work keeps our projects from getting bogged down in confusion or technical debt. On a typical day, you might be cleaning up a project board, assisting a lead developer with documentation, or helping to organize a weekly demo for a client. You will not be expected to write code, but you will be expected to understand how our systems work so you can talk about them accurately. This is a permanent, full\-time role where you will be an essential link between the client requirements and the final product. **What you bring** * A high level of organization and an obsession with lists and clear notes. * The ability to write instructions that are easy for others to read and follow. * A patient approach to repetitive tasks, such as updating ticket statuses or checking data consistency. * Comfort working in a fully remote environment using chat and video calls as your primary base for communication. * An interest in how software is built, even if you do not have a technical degree. * The ability to stay calm and helpful when a project timeline needs to be adjusted. * A direct and honest communication style: we prefer hearing about a problem early rather than late. * The capacity to manage your own time without someone looking over your shoulder. **Helpful background** * Experience using basic project tools like Trello, Jira, or similar task tracking software. * A basic understanding of spreadsheets and how to organize data within them. * Exposure to technical environments, perhaps through a bootcamp or personal project. * Knowledge of what an API is or how a database works at a conceptual level. * Experience in a customer service or administrative role where detail was important. * Familiarity with Google Workspace or similar document collaboration tools. **Working at Pixel Systems** Working here is different from working at a large tech company. We have no complex management layers and no office politics to navigate. We focus entirely on shipping work that helps real people, like operations teams and financial analysts. You will be working directly with senior developers and designers who value your input on how to make our internal processes smoother. Since we are 100 percent remote, we value clear, written communication above all else. We do not have meetings just for the sake of having them. Instead, we use our time to solve problems and get the work done. The position is paid, and we provide a level of compensation that is very competitive for entry\-level roles in the current market. You will have the opportunity to learn how a lean software agency operates from the inside out. We are a small team that values a quiet, focused work environment. We do not chase the latest tech fads or try to reinvent the wheel. We simply build stable systems that solve problems for our clients. If you enjoy bring order to chaos and helping a technical team stay on track, you will fit in well here.

title: Junior Supply Chain Consultant - September 2026
description: **Job Description** **About the role** **Kickstart your consulting career!** Get ready to launch your career in consulting, and join us! You’ll have a spot in our supply chain training boot camp at the beginning of September. You’ll sharpen your consulting expertise alongside fellow new starters from around the globe. Our training program covers everything from essential soft skills to advanced supply chain management to becoming a kick\-ass Supply Chain Consultant. Afterwards, you're ready to start on one of our running projects. **What can you do at Bluecrux?** * You'll start immediately in a visible, autonomous role as a project member in one of our running value chain projects in industries such as life sciences, chemicals, food and beverages, and manufacturing. * You'll see yourself working on and contributing to the main project phases. Here's an idea of what to expect: + Analyze and Optimize: You will always analyze, map, and conduct existing Supply Chain and Operations business processes. + Collaborate: Work closely with clients to understand their needs and make a real impact. Think about preparing and conducting project interviews to understand the client's needs better. You will become aware of the customer organization and be part of the change in their organization and ways of working. + Track and report: You'll track KPIs and report them accurately to the customer project team, ensuring clear, actionable insights. + Document and deliver: You create the most important customer and project deliverables, such as top\-notch project notes, guides and training material. * Here’s how you’ll continuously be making an impact: + Tell us, what’s your flavour? All of our projects share a common transformation mission, and once you join a project, you’ll be introduced to different extra layers of specialisation to add value. We will encourage you to explore and accelerate in Technology, Process knowledge, Data or Change Management. **Job Requirements** **About you** * You will graduate before September 2026 and want to start your career at the beginning of September 2026\. * You see yourself thriving in the world of value chain transformation and are interested in how Technology, Business Processes, Data Analytics, and Change management create a flourishing ecosystem of solutions. * You feel connected with and are ready to represent our company values: dig deep, own it, come together, move fast and be kind! * Above all, we believe in your potential: + You can demonstrate profound logical reasoning, and you are resourceful in detailing solutions and considerate about their consequences. + You're a team worker and player who can perform well without much supervision and who is committed to your own objectives. + You're open to feedback and always look for ways to enhance collaboration. + You like to be challenged daily, show proactive ownership and, above all, thrive in a fast\-paced environment. * Some hard skills: + Ideally, you have had an introduction to Supply Chain Processes in a minor or major within a master’s degree program, such as business engineering, civil engineering—operational research, applied economics, mechanical engineering or an MBA program. + You are fluent in English, the official work language in all of our offices. Mastery of any other language is a definite plus. + Willingness and eligibility to travel globally and to work in a hybrid working model. **Why join us?** * A kickstart to your career through our Supply Chain Bootcamp training program, at the beginning of September. You will learn the ins \& outs of Supply Chain Management: Demand Planning, S\&OP and Financial Planning, Supply Planning, Inventory Management, Master Data Management, Supply Chain Modelling and Planning Tools. This will allow you to onboard into our projects with a backpack full of expertise \& skills! * We know you by your first name, and don't let titles or levels withhold us from being truly aware of your talents. * You'll work on international projects and travel to client sites. From the start, you will gain exposure and recognition for every achievement at Bluecrux. * A Great Place To Work with lots of growth opportunities. We encourage initiative, so you’ll learn a lot quickly. Sometimes, you’ll fail fast, but we’ll help you to get up. We’ll enhance your personal and professional skills, and you can count on a great, supportive team of hands\-on, inspiring and enthusiastic supply chain colleagues. * Team events and fun include yearly Bluecrux weekends, last Friday meetings with drinks, team building, and sports activities. * Enjoy a flexible \& hybrid work environment, combining remote work, office presence (in our brand new office!) and onsite client visits. * A competitive salary package, bonus program, net allowances, a smartphone with phone subscription and other benefits (such as a fridge full of drinks \& healthy snacks). **About us** At Bluecrux, we transform supply chains into smart, efficient value chains through a blend of expert consulting and cutting\-edge technology. We collaborate closely with industry leaders like Johnson \& Johnson, GSK, Bridgestone, AkzoNobel, Beiersdorf, Oatly, and many more – creating partnerships that drive meaningful impact. With a global presence in Europe, the US and APAC, we're committed to shaping the future of value chains, one success story at a time.

title: Junior Technical Analyst - Aviation Advisory
description: The Aviation Advisory team founded in 2016 has since established itself as best in class across its service offerings, growing to a team of 60\+ staff in just 8 years. Current client offerings include; aircraft lease management, data migration management, aircraft maintenance reserve reviews, technical aircraft expertise, deal appraisals for investment decision making, and a broad range of accounting and finance offerings to its clients. The team is currently undertaking lease management for a fleet size of approximately 1,400 leases and provides professional services to 13 out of the Top 15 Aircraft Lessors. **The Opportunity** We have an opportunity for an entry level technical analyst, who is interested in working within the GT Aviation Lease Services team. The successful candidate will be joining a team that has gone through significant growth in recent years with ambitious plans to grow the Aviation Lease Services offering. This is an opportunity for someone with the ability to understand problems quickly to work with the number one provider of Aviation Lease Services to the aircraft leasing industry in Dublin – you will be immersed in the industry with high calibre colleagues. **Roles \& Responsibilities** * Fleet technical records data management, documentation updating and control * Assist with asset review system control and assessments * Manage and administer the technical specification database and reporting * Input monthly utilization and review component status changes * Maintain up to date technical knowledge of each aircraft by type * Input maintain and review fleet’s aircraft specifications, aircraft technical information, equipment data and specifications * Analyzing and updating shop visit events * Assist with MR Claims process **Job Requirements** * Aeronautical Engineering, Aviation Technology Degree or Equivalent * Detail\-Orientated and organized * Meet deadlines and be able to prioritize * Must be diligent, thorough, numerate with a keen attention to detail * Excellent knowledge of Microsoft Excel, Word, Outlook * Previous experience of working in aviation is a distinct advantage

Whether you simply want to say thank you, celebrate a special occasion or give an award for doing something exceptional, you can do all of this and more through the scheme.

title: Legal Counsel
description: **About Newcode.ai** Newcode.ai is a fast\-growing legal tech and agentic AI company transforming how legal work is done. With teams across Norway, Sweden, Ireland, the US and growing we work at the intersection of law, technology, and intelligence. We move fast, think big, and take pride in doing things the right way. **The Role** We're looking for a Legal Counsel to build and scale our legal and compliance capabilities as we continue to grow internationally. This role will serve as a strategic partner to leadership, supporting commercial growth, managing corporate governance, protecting company assets, and ensuring compliance across multiple jurisdictions. **Key Responsibilities** : **Commercial Contracts** * Draft, review, negotiate, and manage customer, vendor, partner, and technology agreements * Partner with Sales and Customer Success to accelerate deal execution while managing legal risk * Develop and maintain contract templates, playbooks, and approval processes to improve efficiency and consistency * Support contract lifecycle management and legal operations initiatives **Corporate \& Governance** * Manage corporate governance activities across multiple entities and jurisdictions * Support equity administration, cap table management, and corporate transactions * Ensure ongoing compliance with local statutory and regulatory requirements **IP \& Compliance** * Maintain and protect the company's intellectual property portfolio, including trademarks, copyrights, and proprietary assets * Support and manage compliance programs, including ISO certifications, data protection, security, and regulatory frameworks * Partner with internal stakeholders to identify, assess, and remediate compliance gaps * Develop and deliver compliance policies, training, and awareness programs * Experience in Vanta, OneTrust or similar software **Risk \& Disputes** * Identify, assess, and mitigate legal, regulatory, operational, and commercial risks * Manage disputes, claims, and litigation matters with external counsel when required * Provide practical legal guidance to business leaders on risk management and strategic decision\-making * Maintain risk registers and support company\-wide risk management initiatives **Requirements** * Law degree from an accredited university in Ireland, Sweden, or Norway * 5\+ years of experience as in house legal counsel preferably at an AI / SaaS tech start ups * Experience supporting commercial contracting and international business operations * Strong understanding of corporate governance, regulatory compliance, and risk management practices * Familiarity with ISO standards, privacy regulations, and information security frameworks is highly desirable * Excellent negotiation, communication, and stakeholder management skills * Ability to balance legal rigor with business pragmatism in a fast\-paced startup environment **Benefits** **Why You'll Love Working with Us** * Culture of trust and excellence: Be part of a team that values reliability, respect, and initiative, where everyone's contribution matters and diversity drives creativity * Collaborative environment: Work with talented, driven peers on bleeding\-edge legal engineering solutions with real\-world impact * Growth and ownership: Shape your role as we grow. Take ownership and drive outcomes from the start of your journey * Inclusive culture of excellence and trust: We reward initiative, respect, integrity, and teamwork, and are committed to building a global, inclusive team. Your drive, curiosity, and ideas matter more than your background Ready to shape what's next? Apply today and join us in building a smarter, more efficient world powered by AI.

title: Logistics Associate
description: **WHO WE ARE:** EOS IT Solutions is a Global Technology and Logistics company, providing Collaboration and Business IT Support services to some of the world’s largest industry leaders, delivering forward\-thinking solutions based on multi\-domain architecture. Customer satisfaction and commitment to superior quality of service are our top business priorities, along with investing in and supporting our partners and employees. We are a true International IT provider and are proud to deliver our services through global simplicity with trusted transparency **THE POSITION** Based at EOS IT Solutions' facility in Swords, Dublin, the Warehouse \& Logistics Associate is primarily responsible for ensuring that day\-to\-day operational logistics activities run effectively and efficiently. This includes cycle counting, inventory receipting, and processing of all inbound and outbound shipments. The successful candidate will work as part of a dynamic and growing team to deliver EOS suppliers’ and customers with an unrivalled service and contribute to our growth and success. **What You Will Do** * Loading and unloading trucks, cycle counting, kitting, picking, packing, shrink wrapping, labelling, and palletizing products. * Receiving and processing all equipment shipments. * Responsible for performing the physical or administrative/physical tasks storing, and distributing of products, materials, parts, supplies and equipment. * Ensure exceptional performance levels on site across all areas of the operation including, but not restricted to quoting, bookings, tracking, compliance and managing freight vendors. * Performs other duties and responsibilities when needed. **ESSENTIAL CRITERIA:** * Basic knowledge of warehouse and logistics operations. * Be an analytical thinker who can help organize and complete tasks. * Be innovative and self\-motivated, with a keen ability to ask questions, as well as a self\-directed learner. * Excellent interpersonal skills, including relationship building and collaboration within a diverse, cross\-functional team. * Ability to pay meticulous attention to detail. **DESIRABLE CRITERIA:** * Previous experience working in a Warehouse/Logistics environment * Forklift License \- preferably Reach Truck * Familiarity of generating AWB’s/shipping documentation **BELONGING AT EOS:** *EOS is committed to creating a diverse and inclusive work environment and is proud to be an equal opportunity employer. We invite you to consider opportunities at EOS regardless of your gender; gender identity; gender reassignment; age; religious or similar philosophical belief; race; national origin; political opinion; sexual orientation; disability; marital or civil partnership status or other non\-merit factor.* \#IND

title: Maintenance Fitter
description: Clerkin Connect is currently recruiting for a Maintenance Fitter on behalf of a well\-established manufacturing client based in Athy, Co. Kildare. This is an excellent opportunity for a qualified mechanical tradesperson to join a busy production environment, where you will play a key role in keeping equipment running safely and reliably. The role will begin on day work, with a move into a 3 or 4 cycle shift pattern over time, including shifts ranging from 8 to 12 hours. There is also genuine opportunity for progression into leadership or management for the right person. **Key responsibilities** * Carry out planned and reactive mechanical maintenance on production machinery to minimise downtime. * Work to preventative maintenance schedules and liaise with planners to ensure work is completed on time. * Diagnose faults and repair mechanical breakdowns to support continuous production. * Complete risk assessments, method statements, and permit\-to\-work requirements as needed. * Support and assist in the training of mechanical apprentices. * Maintain accurate maintenance records and associated documentation. **What we are looking for** * A recognised Mechanical Trades Apprenticeship, or equivalent qualification. * Previous experience in a manufacturing environment is essential, ideally within construction materials, heavy industry, engineering, or a similar maintenance setting. * Strong knowledge of hydraulics, pneumatics, and turning/milling. * A relevant trade qualification is essential. * Previous experience working shift patterns, including continental or other unsociable shift structures. * Good English communication skills, both written and verbal. **Desirable** * Strong organisational skills with a reliable and committed approach to work. * Additional trade\-related training or certifications would be an advantage. **What is on offer** * Opportunity to join a well\-established and reputable manufacturing business. * Long\-term career potential with progression opportunities. * Supportive team environment within a safety\-focused workplace. * The chance to work in a hands\-on, technically varied maintenance role. **Application Process** This role is being managed by Clerkin Connect on behalf of our client. All applications and enquiries will be treated in the strictest confidence.

title: Maintrol Support Engineer
description: **Description** Ryanair are currently recruiting a Maintrol Support Engineer to join the Engineering Team of Europe’s Largest Airline Group. Based in our Head Office in Dublin, this is an excellent opportunity for a recent graduate to gain experience in Europe’s leading airline, working closely with Senior Management responsible for maintenance of over 600 aircraft. Working a part of our Maintenance Control team you will report to the Maintenance Control Manager and work as part of a team with responsibility for ensure that our operation runs as smoothly as possible as we fly 3,600\+ flights per day and 20\+million passengers per month. **Duties Will Include** * Assisting with the return to service of AOG aircraft. * Liaising with Engineers/Flight crew with regard to technical defects. * Dispatch of the Lear Jet to transport engineers and spares to aid AOG recovery. * Review of open HILs and other forecasting maintenance to ensure that tasks are being planned and accomplished as required. * Update and distribution of Tech Issues to provide visibility of fleet AOG status **Requirements** * Relevant degree or Aviation experience * Excellent communications and organisational skills. * Ability to react to changing situations in a calm and controlled manner. * Ability to multi\-task. * Willing to work shifts including night shift **Benefits** * Competitive salary * Discounted and unlimited travel to over 250 destinations * Defined Contribution Pension Scheme – Matched up to 5% or €5,000 * Death in Service Benefit – Up to 2 times of annual basic salary * 20 Days Annual Leave – Increasing to 22 days after 12 months and 25 days after 3 years of continuous service. * Option for up to 5 additional unpaid leave days per year * Cycle 2 Work Scheme * Unrivalled career progression Competencies

title: Managing Consultant
description: *At Fruition Group Ireland we embrace our values of* ***Pride*** *,* ***Energy*** *and* ***Perseverance*** *and our colleagues embed them as the foundation of their work. Our colleagues are proud to work for Fruition Group, they are pro\-active with high energy to maintain our market\-leading status. With a diverse team we recognise that no two people are the same and we focus on results rather than unachievable KPIs and work collaboratively to achieve our goals whilst having fun along the way!* *We are a high growth organisation who work with a huge range of technology employers throughout the UK, Ireland, EU and USA and as a business we re\-invest profits to ensure we are leading in recruitment innovation in our marketplace and equipping our colleagues with the best environment, tools and training to help them succeed.* *Following the great success and growth for our team in Bray we are now opening a new office in Dublin City! This is a great opportunity to join a team on a* ***Managing*** *level with possibilities to lead and be part of a team with both current and new colleagues!* ***The role*** Reporting directly into the Directors, who are some of Irelands biggest billers. You will play a pivotal part in the resourcing of candidates for our clients and the development of new client relationships. You will continue to develop your network of candidates within the tech (perm and contract) space and continually keeping up to date with current trends. You will think outside of the box for sourcing strategies and utilise all internal tech provided to maximise efficiency. There is excellent opportunity to be part of a new office with a successful current business that is rapidly growing globally. Where you get the chance to develop your mentoring and leadership skills, as you will begin to support junior colleagues with their day\-to\-day recruitment. ***Key Responsibilities*** * Development of accounts into key accounts * Searching for suitable Tech candidates who match our clients’ requirements * Headhunting and networking on sites such as LinkedIn / GitHub / Stack Overflow * Writing and posting job adverts * Telephone interviewing candidates to assess their suitability for specific roles * Arranging interviews for candidates and liaising with them during this time * Making job offers to suitable candidates and handling rejections * Taking references / collating relevant supporting documents * Building relationships with candidates to create a strong talent pipeline * Use social media to create a personal brand * Keep up to date with trends within the Tech industry * Building relationships with existing and future clients * Utilise internal tech such as SourceWhale, LinkedHelper and Paiger * Ensure all data is accurately recorded on CRM * Attending meeting with clients to explore potential opportunities * Negotiating fees and terms of engagement * Manage the team \& support junior colleagues with their day\-to\-day recruitment ***Key Skills and Capabilities*** * Outstanding communicational skills with the ability to adapt language to different audiences * Ability to build long—lasting professional relationships * Ability to handle difficult conversations * Excellent analytical and problem\-solving skills, attention to detail; well organised * Proactive and independent thinker * Excellent customer service skills * Strong presentation skills with the ability to convey key messages to diverse and wide audiences * Exceptional Stakeholder Engagement skills * Experience working to targets * Great negotiating skills * Excellent Experience using the telephone in a professional environment is desirable * Great understanding of technical recruitment * Experience within a sales environment is desirable ***Your Benefits to name a few:*** * **Competitive basic salary with uncapped earning potential** * **Lucrative commission – no thresholds!** * **Ongoing training with external learning providers** * **Clear and structured Career progression** * **Biannual winners trip to European destinations such as Marbella, Barcelona \& Amsterdam** * **Flexible working offer \- between the office and home** * **Quarterly lunch clubs for top achievers** * **Quarterly team socials** * **Friday early finish** * **Private healthcare** * **Pension Contribution** If you’re interested in joining Fruition as Managing Consultant, please get in touch for an initial confidential chat about our available opportunities, and we’ll happily share our story with you. For further information please email lois.gooding@fruitiongroup.com We are an equal opportunities employer and welcome applications from all suitably qualified persons regardless of their race, sex, disability, religion/belief, sexual orientation or age.

title: Manufacturing Systems Specialist
description: As a Manufacturing Systems specialist you will play a key role in establishing the Manufacturing Systems team within the Manufacturing Operations group. The incumbent will provide expert support to the manufacturing team in the areas of SAP, LIMS, finite scheduling (Orchestrate) and benchtop equipment. **Essential Duties and Responsibilities** * Key member of Manufacturing Operations team in WuXi Biologics Ireland. * Provide expert support to the Manufacturing department in the areas of SAP, LIMS, Trackwise and Finite Scheduling. * Process and System owner for Manufacturing benchtop equipment. Responsibilities in this area will include but are not limited to management of the MFG benchtop support e.g. vendors, maintain an accurate equipment list of benchtop equipment and ensure all required system SOPs are in place. * Ensure all Manufacturing benchtop equipment are maintained in a qualified state by collaborating with relevant departments on all aspects of benchtop equipment qualification lifecycle (URS to decommissioning). * Co\-ordinate vendor visits on site for bench top support, qualification and maintenance as needed. * Provide input to budget for systems. * Collaborate with MT and MSAT groups on technical transfer and process validation as required. * Collaborate with QC \& QA groups to ensure GMP compliance, manage deviation investigation and CAPA implementation, and support client auditing and agency inspections. * Ensure that the MFG training team are supported in regards to benchtop equipment training and troubleshooting as needed. * Ensuring continual improvement of manufacturing documentation (SOP’s MBR’s, OJT’s) for areas of responsibility to ensure they are current, accurate, and clear. * Communicate operational status regularly to senior management, as required, at the appropriate level of detail. * Assist the MFG systems lead to coordinate the work between the MFG systems groups and other functional groups to meet the production objectives and timelines. * Perform all duties in accordance with GMP requirements, SOPs and controlled documents * Responsible for identifying and assessing new models of benchtop and new equipment as required. * Will be flexible to take on additional tasks and responsibilities at the discretion of the Manufacturing Operations Associate Director and Systems lead. * Support future initiatives such as electronic batch records, electronic logbooks, etc. on behalf of Manufacturing Will act as a role model for the Manufacturing function and also the wider organisation in adherence to the WuXi corporate core values and PROUD culture. **Person Specification** **Technical Competencies** Ideally, experienced in establishing a Manufacturing department and working in a team in a ‘greenfield’ context of similar size and scale. **Experience** * More than 2 years in biopharmaceutical manufacturing. * Experience with GMP systems such as SAP and Benchtop equipment qualification and operation etc. * Ideally have a solid understanding of quality system in terms of deviation, investigation, CAPA and change control. * Experience in working with the cross\-functional teams. * Strong team lead with the demonstrated ability to manage and support manufacturing operations in a flexible and dynamic team environment. **Knowledge** * A proficiency in operating a drug substance commercial manufacturing facility and/or a pilot plant. * The individual need to have good understanding in DQ, FAT, SAT, IQ, OQ, \& PQ (commissioning and qualification). * Knowledge of GMP systems such as SAP and Benchtop equipment qualification and operation etc. * Ideally knowledge and experience of scheduling software including Orchestrate. **Qualifications** * Diploma, BSc, MSc or PhD in Chemical Engineering, Bioscience, Biotechnology or Analytical Science **Behavioural Competencies** * A strong team player with the ability to adapt to daily challenges of a manufacturing work environment while maintaining positive and productive attitude and fostering this attitude among direct reports. * Excellent verbal and written communication and presentation skills. * Excellent interpersonal skills with the ability to work effectively with staff at all levels of the organisation. * Ability to work on own initiative and be proactive. * Results oriented, with the ability to manage multiple priorities in a short period of time. * Excellent organisational and planning skills and strong attention to detail. As we extend our global reach, we need colleagues interested in change, in challenge, and in building a new future together. Reach beyond yourself and discover your true potential! If you can thrive in a dynamic and fast\-paced environment and feel that the moment has come for you to be part of such an exciting journey towards accomplishing our mission \- Any drug can be made, and any disease can be treated \- don’t miss out on this opportunity to join us and reach beyond yourself and discover your true potential. Apply now! Would you like to know more before you apply? Please visit us at https://www.wuxibiologics.com/join\-us/ WuXi Biologics is an equal opportunities employer.

internet speeds, Wi\-Fi problems, and network security threats. * **Cable Management:** Organize and label cables to ensure efficient airflow and accessibility. **Customer Service Skills:** * **Effective Communication:** Clearly explaining technical concepts to non\-technical customers. Excellent verbal and written communication skills. * **Problem\-Solving:** Identifying and resolving customer issues efficiently and accurately. * **Patience and Empathy:** Ability to build professional rapport with clients and maintain positive relationships. * Attention to detail and strong organizational skills. **Time Management:** * Efficient time management to meet deadlines and optimize work schedules. **Additional Requirements:** * Valid driver's license and reliable transportation. * Ability to lift and carry heavy equipment. * Ability to use ladder for troubleshooting access points and cabling. * Willingness to work flexible hours, including occasional evenings, weekends, and holidays. * Up to 30 \-40% country wide travel. **Benefits** * A supportive, diverse, and global team with a brilliant culture. * Competitive compensation and benefits that includes up to 20 days’ vacation per year, various insurances like Term life and Business Travel insurance. These are apart from the statutory benefits applicable in the country. Employee benefits are regulated by an internal policy that contains full details regarding the entitlement and conditions for the benefits as per the law of the land. * Great opportunities to make the role your own, upskill yourself and get involved with exciting projects. * Total Wellbeing is our focus. Alongside your professional excellence, you join the likeminded colleagues to create a larger impact within the company and society at large in your chosen area of passion \- CSR Council, Diversity Council, Women Connect, Sparks – Engagement Champion to name a few. For more information on how we process your personal data, please refer to HCLTech’s **Candidate Data Privacy Notice .** To know more about us visit – www.hcltech.com

title: Office Assistant
description: **About the Role** We're looking for a *part\-time Office Assistant* to join our Dublin office 6\-month contract. This is an exciting opportunity for someone who wants to build experience in a dynamic, high\-growth fintech environment and play a meaningful role in keeping our Dublin office running smoothly. Working 2 days per week, you'll be the go\-to person for day\-to\-day office operations, making everything run efficiently behind the scenes. **What You'll Be Responsible For** * Keeping the office fully stocked and well\-presented — from kitchen supplies and equipment to stationery and storage * Acting as the first point of contact for visitors, deliveries, and building management queries * Booking and coordinating meeting rooms, ensuring spaces are set up and ready for use * Leading the coordination of team socials and events, and supporting the team on company\-wide activities * Managing incoming and outgoing post and couriers * Supporting new starter onboarding logistics, including desk and laptop set\-up, access passes, and welcome packs * Managing the relationship with our building landlord and external vendors to keep day\-to\-day operations running smoothly * Providing general admin support to the Operations team as needed **What We're Looking For** * Some prior experience in an office assistant, receptionist, or admin support role * Highly organised with strong attention to detail * Reliable and consistent — the kind of person colleagues can count on * Proactive and self\-motivated; you spot what needs doing and can use your initiative to get it done * Warm and professional in your communication with colleagues and external visitors * Comfortable with everyday tech — Google Workspace or Microsoft Office, and happy to troubleshoot basics like WiFi, laptop set\-up, and monitor connections * Available to work in\-office 2 days per week in Dublin, and seeking part\-time hours **About Titanbay** At Titanbay we are on a mission to open up private market investing for wealth managers, private banks, and their customers. We are deeply committed to delivering unmatched value and service to our clients by offering innovative solutions that empower them to navigate private markets with confidence and success. Our ethos revolves around customer obsession and our ability to solve difficult problems well for our customers. We foster a culture of transparency, integrity, and accountability, where every team member is empowered to take ownership, act with urgency, and earn the trust of colleagues, clients, and partners. Join us on our journey to reshape the future of private market investing and unlock new opportunities for wealth managers and investors alike.

title: Operations Administrator
description: At **ABB**, we help industries run leaner and cleaner—and every person here makes that happen. You’ll be empowered to lead, supported to grow, and proud of the impact we create together. Join us and help run what runs the world. **This position reports to:** Service Operations Manager \_\_ **Job Summary** In this role, you will work with the Operations Manager to provide general administrative services at the business level and provide back\-up support within the Operations team within Electrification Service (ELSE) division. This role will be a 12 month fixed term contract. **Our Team Dynamics** Our teams support each other, collaborate, and never stop learning. Everyone brings something unique, and together we push ideas forward to solve real problems. Being part of our team means your work matters \- because the progress we make here creates real impact out there. **What’s in it for you?** We want you to bring your full self to work—your ideas, your energy, your ambition. You’ll have the tools and freedom to grow your skills, shape your path, and take on challenges that matter. Here, your work creates impact you can see and feel, every day. **Your Role and Responsibilities** In this role, you’ll help run what runs the world, by taking on meaningful work that drives real impact. *Work model: \#Hybrid from our Dublin office* * Performs general administrative activities and provides backup support to the team in case of sickness/absence/workload. * Acts as local support for all administration activities within ELSE Ireland. * Schedules and coordinates training for the Field Service Engineers to enable them to proceed to a customer visit safely. * Schedules Inductions and access to ABB Customer sites * Builds and maintains strong relationships with internal and external stakeholders and effectively communicates with all stakeholders. **Qualifications for the Role** * Minimum 3 years’ experience in a business administration support role or similar with proven ability to manage multiple tasks and priorities * Excellent attention to detail, ability to maintain accurate records, and proficiency with office management systems. * Competent use of Microsoft Office Suite (Word, Excel, Outlook), knowledge of SAP advantageous * Strong written and verbal communication abilities in English, with capability to interact professionally with all levels of staff. * Ability to identify issues, take initiative, work independently, and contribute ideas for improving operational efficiency. **Benefits** As part of our dedication to our employees, ABB Ireland offers an attractive array of benefits which include * Competitive Salary * Private Medical Allowance for Yourself and Family * Generous Bonus Scheme * Competitive Contributory Pension Scheme * Employee Share Acquisition Plan * Open Job Market (Internal Mobility Opportunities, Mentorships \& Access To Projects Globally) * Employee Assistance Programme * Generous Employee Referral Scheme * 25 Days Annual Leave Plus Bank Holidays **More about us** Join ABB Ireland and be part of a dynamic team that is making a real difference. We offer a supportive and inclusive work environment where you can grow your career, develop your skills, and contribute to impactful work that matters. Together, we are building a sustainable future for generations to come. **More about us** ABB's Service Division partners with our customers to improve the availability, reliability, predictability and sustainability of electrical products and installations. The Division’s extensive service portfolio offers product care, modernization, and advisory services to improve performance, extend equipment lifetime and deliver new levels of operational and sustainable efficiency. We help customers keep resources in use for as long as possible, extracting the maximum value from them, and then recovering and regenerating products and materials at the end of their useful life. Join us. Be part of the team where progress happens, industries transform, and your work shapes the world. \#RunWhatRunstheWorld. Building a cleaner, smarter future takes all kinds of minds: the curious, the courageous, and the creative. That's why we welcome people from all backgrounds and experiences. Ready to make an impact? Apply today or visit **https://www.abb.com** to learn more about the impact of our solutions across the globe.

title: Optical Deployment Engineer
description: **Description** As an Optical Deployment Engineer working within AWS Infrastructure you will: * Build and rollout our global DWDM networks * Drive scaling current DWDM designs to meet the demand of our customers * Create processes that help operate and build our DWDM network * Work closely with other Networking departments on new designs * Evaluate alternative equipment and drive commercial tender process * Ensure that the DWDM network adheres to the defined network standards * Review and implement changes on the DWDM network * Drive operational excellence projects to drive improvements in our DWDM network quality and reliability * Troubleshoot complex problems and develop innovative solutions day to day on our network. * Our ideal candidate is highly autonomous, possesses strong written and verbal communication skills, strong project management and time management skills and has a successful track record of troubleshooting complex network problems and delivering innovative solutions. The desire and ability to work in a fast paced, collaborative environment is essential. This is an amazing opportunity in terms of responsibility, interesting challenges and high visibility. We truly are looking for the highest quality candidates, so you should expect a rigorous technical interview process. Key job responsibilities Build and rollout our global DWDM networks Drive scaling current DWDM designs to meet the demand of our customers Create processes that help operate and build our DWDM network Work closely with other Networking departments on new designs Evaluate alternative equipment and drive commercial tender process Ensure that the DWDM network adheres to the defined network standards Review and implement changes on the DWDM network Drive operational excellence projects to drive improvements in our DWDM network quality and reliability Troubleshoot complex problems and develop innovative solutions day to day on our network. Our ideal candidate is highly autonomous, possesses strong written and verbal communication skills, strong project management and time management skills and has a successful track record of troubleshooting complex network problems and delivering innovative solutions. The desire and ability to work in a fast paced, collaborative environment is essential. **About The Team** We are a diverse, collaborative team of network, software, and hardware engineers dedicated to maintaining the backbone of cloud infrastructure. Our team thrives on solving complex challenges, pushing technological boundaries, and creating solutions that support global connectivity. We value innovation, inclusivity, and empowering team members to own their ideas from conception to implementation. Why AWS Amazon Web Services (AWS) is the world’s most comprehensive and broadly adopted cloud platform. We pioneered cloud computing and never stopped innovating — that’s why customers from the most successful startups to Global 500 companies trust our robust suite of products and services to power their businesses. Work/Life Balance We value work\-life harmony. Achieving success at work should never come at the expense of sacrifices at home, which is why we strive for flexibility as part of our working culture. When we feel supported in the workplace and at home, there’s nothing we can’t achieve. Inclusive Team Culture Here at AWS, it’s in our nature to learn and be curious. Our employee\-led affinity groups foster a culture of inclusion that empower us to be proud of our differences. Ongoing events and learning experiences, including our Conversations on Race and Ethnicity (CORE) and AmazeCon conferences, inspire us to never stop embracing our uniqueness. Mentorship and Career Growth We’re continuously raising our performance bar as we strive to become Earth’s Best Employer. That’s why you’ll find endless knowledge\-sharing, mentorship and other career\-advancing resources here to help you develop into a better\-rounded professional. **Basic Qualifications** * Bachelor's degree or equivalent qualification in Math, Engineering, Science or Business * Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Engineering, a related field, or equivalent experience * Experience with the rollout of large DWDM networks and Optical network technologies * Experience with optical planning and modelling tools * Professional development experience with Python or similar tool for network automation scripting **Preferred Qualifications** * Knowledge of network analysis fundamentals and robust troubleshooting * Knowledge of IP networking fundamentals and extensive experience in the application of IP protocols * Experience with programming/scripting (Batch, VB, PowerShell, Java, C\#, Chef, Perl, Ruby and/or PHP), or experience in Ubuntu/Solaris and experience that includes strong analytical skills, attention to detail, and effective communication abilities Amazon is an equal opportunities employer. title: PMO & Automation Analyst - Technology (IT PMO)
description: **Join us** At Vodafone, we’re not just shaping the future of connectivity for our customers — we’re shaping the future for everyone who joins our team. When you work with us, you’re part of a global mission to connect people, solve complex challenges, and create a sustainable and more inclusive world. If you want to grow your career whilst finding the right balance between work and life, Vodafone offers the opportunity to help you belong and make a real impact. **Role Summary** As a **PMO \& Automation Analyst** , you will lead the transformation of project governance processes through automation and data\-driven insights. You will work closely with Project Managers, Finance, and our offshore VOIS teams to implement AI\-driven workflows, Power Automate solutions, and interactive dashboards that streamline project financial management and reporting. Your role is to reduce manual effort and improve efficiency by replacing traditional administrative tasks with smart, automated processes. This is an exciting opportunity for a proactive, tech\-savvy PMO professional with a passion for innovation, data, and continuous improvement within a dynamic enterprise environment. **What You Will Do** **Key Responsibilities:\-** **Design \& Manage Automated Workflows:** Develop and maintain automated processes (using tools like Power Automate and other AI/ML technologies) for key project governance activities, such as resource time tracking, approvals, and financial updates – ensuring these workflows run smoothly and minimize manual intervention. **Financial Governance \& Budget Tracking:** Partner with Project Managers and Finance to maintain accurate, real\-time project budgets and forecasts. Use digital tools and dashboards to track spend, forecast costs, and ensure resources are allocated efficiently. **Offshore (VOIS) Budget Coordination:** Coordinate with offshore/VOIS teams to manage and track budgets for outsourced project components. Ensure seamless integration of offshore costs into overall project financials and that all expenses are properly approved and documented. **Dynamic Reporting \& Dashboards:** Create and refine interactive dashboards and management reports (e.g., in Power BI or similar tools) that give senior stakeholders clear, real\-time visibility of project status, risks, and key metrics. Continuously improve these reporting outputs using feedback and new data techniques. **Advanced Analytics \& Insights:** Perform ad\-hoc data analysis on project and portfolio data to identify trends, anomalies, or improvement opportunities. Present insights through high\-quality visuals and presentations (e.g., slides, charts) to support decision\-making by leadership. **Governance \& Standards Ownership:** Drive consistency and best practices across the project management community by developing and promoting standard templates, automated processes, and data governance standards. Ensure that project teams adhere to these standards for efficient, aligned ways of working. **Continuous Improvement \& Innovation:** Proactively identify process inefficiencies or manual work steps in project and financial management. Conceive and implement improvements – from small process tweaks to larger automation initiatives – that increase productivity and reduce errors. **Stakeholder Engagement \& Training:** Act as a champion for new tools and processes, coaching and supporting project teams in adopting automated solutions. Provide training or guidance to colleagues on using new dashboards and workflow tools, building a culture of data\-driven decision making. **What You’ll Need** **Skills \& Experience:** **Technical Proficiency:** Hands\-on experience with (or strong aptitude to learn) automation and analytics tools such as Microsoft Power Automate, Power BI, or similar workflow/BI platforms. Ability to design simple automation scripts or use AI\-based tools to streamline processes. **Financial \& Analytical Skills:** Solid understanding of project budgeting, forecasting, and cost management principles. Comfortable working with financial data and large datasets, demonstrating strong analysis and numeracy skills with high attention to detail and accuracy. **Project \& Tool Knowledge:** Familiarity with project management processes and common tools (e.g., SharePoint, Microsoft Project or similar PM software). Proficiency with Microsoft Office suite, especially Excel for data manipulation and PowerPoint for presentation of complex information. **Problem\-Solving \& Innovation:** A proactive, curious mindset with proven ability to identify process gaps and implement creative technical solutions. Willingness to continuously learn and experiment with new technologies (AI, bots, data analytics) to improve ways of working. **Self\-Management \& Collaboration:** Ability to work independently, managing multiple priorities to deliver high\-quality outputs with minimal supervision. Excellent communication and interpersonal skills – able to work effectively with cross\-functional teams, explain technical concepts in simple terms, and influence stakeholders to embrace new processes. **Desirable** **PMO/Project Experience:** Previous experience in a Project Management Office, IT project delivery, or digital transformation support role, especially within a large or complex organisation. **Automation Credentials:** Exposure to robotic process automation (RPA), simple scripting (e.g., VBA, Python), or AI\-driven applications in a business context. Any relevant certification in Microsoft Power Platform or data analytics would be an advantage. **Global/Offshore Collaboration:** Experience working with offshore or remote teams and an understanding of cultural dynamics in a global organisation. **Industry Knowledge:** Familiarity with telecommunications or technology projects and understanding of their common challenges. **What We’re Looking For:** **A Tech\-Enthusiastic Problem Solver:** A motivated, detail\-oriented individual who gets excited about leveraging technology and data to drive efficiency and improve processes. **A Collaborative Influencer:** Someone who enjoys engaging with diverse stakeholders (from Project Managers to senior leaders and offshore colleagues) and can influence others to adopt new tools and practices through clear communication and demonstration of value. **A Change Champion:** A team player who embraces innovation and values governance, balancing consistency with continuous improvement. You are not afraid to challenge the status quo and can navigate change positively, acting as a champion for new ways of working. **A Growth Mindset:** An individual keen to develop their skills in automation, data analysis, and project governance within a large, dynamic enterprise. You thrive on learning new technologies and have the adaptability to evolve with the role as the organisation continues its digital transformation. **Not a perfect fit?** Worried that you don’t meet all the desired criteria exactly? At Vodafone we are passionate about empowering people and creating a workplace where everyone can thrive, whatever their personal or professional background. If you’re excited about this role but your experience doesn’t align exactly with every part of the job description, we encourage you to still apply — you may be the right candidate for this role or another opportunity. **What’s in it for you?** * The flexibility to work how you perform best, with hybrid ways of working and real focus on work\-life balance * A competitive package including salary, bonus and a strong pension to support you now and long\-term * ''Generous'' flexible benefits allowance, plus access to comprehensive health and wellbeing support * Access to our on\-site wellness centre and gym, alongside a wider wellbeing programme * Free mobile plan, device offers and exclusive discounts for you, your family and friends * Market\-leading family policies and flexibility to support you at every stage of life * Real opportunities for career growth through learning, development and internal progression **Who We Are** We are a leading international Telco, serving millions of customers. At Vodafone, we believe that connectivity is a force for good. If we use it for the things that really matter, it can improve people’s lives and the world around us. Through our technology, we empower people — connecting everyone regardless of who they are or where they live — and we protect the planet, whilst helping our customers do the same. Belonging at Vodafone isn’t a concept; it’s lived, breathed, and cultivated through everything we do. You’ll be part of a global and diverse community with many different minds, abilities, backgrounds and cultures. We’re committed to increasing diversity, ensuring equal representation, and making Vodafone a place where everyone feels safe, valued and included. Vodafone is committed to supporting candidates with disabilities during the recruitment process. If you require any adjustments, please let us know. **Together we can.**

title: PMO Analyst - EPOS - 12 Month FTC
description: Because we strive to put people first. Culture, our way **Technology at Primark** Our technology team is actively shaping the next wave of advancements. Engaged with innovative initiatives, your expertise will propel our business into the future. Collaborating with a creative team of tech enthusiasts, you’ll contribute your unique skills to fuel our technological advancements. We are seeking a highly capable PMO Analyst to support the Programme Management Office (PMO) function for a large\-scale EPOS transformation across a global fast fashion retail business. This individual will be responsible for maintaining robust programme controls, reporting frameworks, resource governance, and delivery standards across the programme lifecycle. The ideal candidate will bring senior PMO experience from complex, multi\-workstream technology or retail transformation programmes. * Lead the day\-to\-day operation of the PMO function across the EPOS transformation * Enforce programme governance standards, processes, and reporting cycles adhering to Primark policies and Delivery Governance Framework * Own the programme’s RAID (Risks, Assumptions, Issues, Dependencies) framework and ensure effective tracking and escalation * Oversee integrated planning, milestone tracking, resource forecasting, and dependency management * Manage programme reporting to steering groups, exec stakeholders, and senior leadership * Support programme/quality assurance across all delivery activities, including stage gates and audit readiness * Proven 5\+ years minimum experience supporting PMO functions in large\-scale, complex programmes (ideally retail or EPOS\-related) * Exceptional skills in governance, planning, RAID management, and stakeholder reporting * Experience in multi\-vendor, multi\-geography delivery environments * Advanced skills in Microsoft Project, Excel, PowerPoint, and project portfolio tools (e.g. Planisware, Clarity, or similar) * Strong interpersonal, facilitation, and communication skills at all levels **Desirable:** * PRINCE2, MSP, P3O or similar certifications * Background in technology or retail transformation **About Primark** At Primark, people matter. They’re the beating heart of our business and the reason we’ve grown from our first store in Dublin in 1969 to a £9bn\+ turnover business and over 80,000 colleagues and over 440 stores in 17 countries today. Our values run through everything we do. In essence, we're Caring and always strive to put people first. We're also Dynamic, bravely pushing the boundaries to stay ahead. And finally, we succeed Together. If you need any reasonable adjustments or have an accessibility request, during your recruitment journey, such as extended time or breaks between online assessments, a sign language interpreter, mobility access, or assistive technology please contact your talent acquisition specialist. All offers of employment are subject to background checks, including right to work, reference education and for some roles criminal, and financial checks. If you have any concerns, please reach out to our talent acquisition team to discuss. **REQ ID:** JR\-10476

title: Packaging Operative
description: **Packaging Operative \- Avoca Central Bakery \& Kitchen Bray** Avoca operates in 13 locations across the country. After all this time, the ethos of Avoca remains the same. We cherish our time\-honoured traditions, which have been lovingly passed down through several generations. Our skills might be steeped in a long and rich tradition, but our attitude is to look to the future. We are now heralded as one of Ireland's most exciting retail stores, with a host of award\-winning cafes, restaurants and food markets crammed with artisanal ingredients from near and far. **Job Summary:** The Packing Operative will be responsible for the day\-to\-day function of the CBK packaging activity. This role is a highly focused on food packaging. A Food Packaging Operative prepares, packs, and labels food products on production, ensuring quality standards and safety regulations (HACCP) are met. Responsibilities include operating machinery, inspecting products for defects, maintaining workstation hygiene, and working efficiently in fast\-paced, between ambient and chilled environments. Requires attention to detail, teamwork, and manual dexterity. **Responsibilities:** * Efficiently pack, seal, and label food products, often at high speeds. * Inspect items for defects, damage, or contamination before packaging. * Set up, operate, and make minor adjustments to packing machinery. * Adhere to strict health, safety, and hygiene standards (PPE required, such as hairnets, bear snood, gloves, coats, safety shoes). * Keep work areas and machinery clean and sanitized. * Complete daily paperwork, including packaging and labelling records. * Move packed goods to racks, dispatch crates or storage areas. **Requirements:** * Attention to Detail. Ability to spot quality issues and follow exact specifications. * Ability to stand for long periods and perform repetitive manual tasks. * Work effectively with colleagues and report faults to supervisors. * Basic skills for reading instructions, labelling, and checking quantities. * Strong work ethic with a focus on punctuality and attendance. * Requires no formal experience (entry\-level), with on\-the\-job training provided. * Previous experience in manufacturing, food, or warehouse roles is advantageous Ability to work in a fast\-paced environment to meet deadlines **Benefits:** * Staff Discount * Employee Assistance Programme * Day time hours Monday \- Friday 9am\-6pm * Cycle to Work Scheme * Pension Scheme

title: Part Time Sales Assistant
description: **Company Description** Sports Direct is the UK's No.1 sports retailer, with an unbeatable range of sportswear, footwear, and equipment at prices that keep customers coming back. With 600\+ stores and still growing, we're part of the Frasers Group: a bold, ambitious business known for shaking up the industry and creating real opportunities at every level. If you've got energy, drive, and a love for retail done at pace, you'll fit right in. **The Role: Sales Assistant** This is retail with momentum. As a Sales Assistant at Sports Direct, you're on the front line of one of the UK's busiest store networks, helping customers find what they need, keeping the floor sharp, and powering the team through every trading day. No two shifts look the same, and that's exactly the point. **What's In It For You** * **Competitive hourly rate** that rewards what you put in * **Flexible working** to fit around your life * **Staff discount** across the full Frasers Group family of brands * **Career progression** in a fast\-growing retail business that promotes from within * **A fun, fast\-paced atmosphere** with a team that has your back **Job Description** **What You'll Do** * Greet customers, answer questions, and help them find the right product. * Deliver brilliant service at every touchpoint, from the door to the till. * Keep the shop floor tidy, well\-stocked, and visually on point. * Replenish shelves and rails so the floor is always ready for the next customer. * Process transactions quickly and accurately. * Promote current offers and spot smart upsell opportunities. * Work as one team to hit daily sales and service goals. **Qualifications** **What You'll Bring** * A confident, friendly, positive attitude. * Strong communication skills and a customer\-first mindset. * A team\-player mentality that thrives when the pace picks up. * Flexibility and reliability, especially through peak trade. * Motivation to deliver results and take real pride in your work. * Retail or customer service experience is a bonus, but not essential. We'll back you to learn. **Additional Information** **How We Work** Our values shape every shift, every conversation, every decision: * **Think Without Limits:** Move fast, take bold action, and bring the team with you. * **Own It and Back Yourself:** Take responsibility, master the basics, and drive results. * **Be Relevant:** Stay connected to our people, our partners, and the planet. Interest in these roles is high, so we may close the vacancy early. Get your application in soon to avoid missing out. **Apply now** and join a winning team at Sports Direct, where passion meets performance.

title: Payroll Analyst
description: **Payroll Analyst – EMEA** **Location:** Dublin (Hybrid – 3 days per week in office) **Contract:** 6\-Month Contract We are seeking an experienced Payroll Analyst to support payroll operations across multiple EMEA countries. This role is ideal for a payroll professional with strong international payroll experience, excellent attention to detail, and the ability to work effectively with internal stakeholders and external payroll providers in a fast\-paced environment **.** **Key Responsibilities** * Manage end\-to\-end payroll processing across multiple EMEA countries, ensuring accuracy, compliance, and timely delivery * Partner with payroll providers and internal teams to resolve payroll queries and support payroll operations * Ensure compliance with local payroll legislation, tax regulations, and statutory reporting requirements * Maintain payroll controls and support audit requirements * Analyse payroll data, perform reconciliations, and prepare reports for stakeholders * Identify and implement process improvements to enhance payroll efficiency and accuracy * Monitor payroll vendor performance and ensure service levels are met * Support payroll\-related projects, system enhancements, and operational initiatives * Stay up to date with payroll legislation and regulatory changes across the EMEA region **Requirements** * Minimum 4 years' experience in international payroll operations * Proven experience processing end\-to\-end payroll across multiple countries * Strong understanding of EMEA payroll legislation, taxation, and compliance requirements * Experience This way, our team can work on high\-impact projects they’ll love, knowing they have the flexibility to manage their personal and professional lives effectively. People\-first \- Great solutions start with great people. At our employee\-led company, your voice matters, and we love to hear your feedback. By leading with kindness, empathy, and striving for equity, we create better experiences for everyone. Our culture encourages passion, adventure, adaptability, and diverse thinking. Inclusion, diversity, and equity are our top priorities, empowering our team to be their best selves. Together, we can create a great future by working collaboratively and supporting each other. Rewards \-There’s no shying away from it \- the compensation and benefits on offer have to be competitive too, right? We know that. That’s why we have a dedicated team working with our leaders to ensure our packages are fair, competitive, and rewarding! Who are we... We are Slalom! Slalom is a next\-generation professional services company creating value at the intersection of business, technology, and humanity. With our fiercely human approach, we deeply understand our customers—and their customers—to deliver practical, end\-to\-end solutions that drive meaningful impact. Supported by over 700 technology partners, our 10,000\+ team members in 11 countries and 52 offices we Inspire people and organisations to envision greater possibilities, accelerate progress, and shape a brighter future for everyone. Want to learn more? Why not apply?? We would love to hear from you! Slalom prides itself on helping our team members thrive in their work and life. As a result, Slalom is proud to invest in our team members with competitive and innovative benefit programs and services that meet the unique needs of our diverse workforce. Будет плюсом: * Опыт С\#. * Kafka/RabbitMQ, интеграционное тестирование. * gRPC. * Docker, Kubernetes/OpenShift. * OAuth2/OIDC/LDAP. * Test management: TestRail/Zephyr/Qase. * Опыт с микросервисной архитектурой. * Конкурентная заработная плата (обсуждается после прохождения собеседования); * График 5/2 c 09:00 до 18:00 или 10\.00 до 19\.00; * Удаленный формат работы; * Выдаем мощные рабочие ноутбуки; * 100% компенсация больничных листов; * Корпоративная скидка на изучение английского языка в Skyeng

This is a high\-impact role within the Quality organisation, offering the opportunity to contribute directly to operational excellence and patient impact. Requirements Key Responsibilities: Provide technical support for Quality Control testing activities, including: Method execution troubleshooting Method establishment Input into investigations Capability building in Biochemistry, Cell Biology, and Analytical methods Support the performance and optimisation of advanced assays requiring strong analytical and scientific understanding. Review, evaluate, and approve analytical testing data. Complete special project and protocol testing in a timely manner under direction of Project Leads and/or Area Leads. Use laboratory instrumentation and Global LIMS or other computerized systems to collect, record, and approve test data/results. Collaborate with laboratory and cross\-functional teams to improve efficiency, solve problems, generate cost savings, and support new product introductions. Operate with minimal supervision and demonstrate sound judgment and accountability. Maintain the laboratory in a GMP\-compliant state in accordance with EHS and 5S requirements. Prepare and update SOPs as required. Apply Lean principles, including 5S, simplification, and standard work, in daily activities. Ensure training requirements are current and support the training of others as needed. Coach and develop team members to build technical capability and support a high\-performance culture. Support regulatory submissions, inspection readiness, health authority inspections, and responses to inspection observations. Contribute to audit readiness, laboratory equipment qualification, and analytical method transfer, verification, and validation. Support site metrics and KPI achievement, implementing countermeasures where required. Maintain data integrity and ensure compliance with company SOPs, specifications, FDA, cGMP, and other applicable regulations. Participate actively in inspections, GEMBAs, and Go\-Sees. Promote a strong safety culture by identifying hazards, conducting risk assessments, and supporting continuous improvement in EHS practices. Act as a visible leader in safety, quality, and continuous improvement initiatives. Encourage innovation, problem solving, and real\-time data\-driven decision\-making to improve the value stream. Perform other tasks as required. What You’ll Bring: Degree in Science, Quality, or a related technical discipline. Minimum of 5 years’ experience in the biotechnology and/or pharmaceutical industry, preferably both. Strong technical knowledge of cell\-based and cell biology methods, such as qPCR, as well as analytical methodologies. Strong understanding of GMP, ICH, USP, and global compendia regulations and guidance, particularly relating to analytical method development and validation.
